nodes.title: Fill in PG contract test bodies (deferred from 14.9)
nodes.description: MTIX-14.9 shipped the test harness scaffolding (providers, secret redaction, CI integration, quirks tests). The 10 contract test bodies in e2e/postgres/contract_test.go are stubs that only call Ping; the full assertions promised by the test names (CRUD, multi-value filters, concurrent mutations, advisory-lock single-flight, audit log triggers, audit-log atomicity, node_type canonicalization, SQL injection resistance, TLS verify-full, statement timeout) require the PG store driver (14.1) and audit_log schema (14.2) to exist first. This ticket fills in the actual assertions once those land. Blocked by 14.1 and 14.2.
nodes.prompt:
SCOPE

Fill in the 10 contract test bodies in e2e/postgres/contract_test.go that were left as Ping-only stubs in MTIX-14.9. Once 14.1 (PG store driver) and 14.2 (audit_log table) land, the contract surface exists and meaningful assertions can be written.

PRECONDITION

This ticket is BLOCKED until both:
- MTIX-14.1: PG store driver registered via RegisterPGStore
- MTIX-14.2: audit_log table schema available via store migrations

DELIVERABLES

For each of the 10 contract tests in e2e/postgres/contract_test.go, replace the Ping-only body with assertions that exercise the behavior described in the test name:

1. TestStore_CRUDContract
   - Create a Node, GetNode, UpdateNode (title/status), DeleteNode (soft)
   - Verify each transition leaves the store in the documented state
   - Round-trip content_hash matches

2. TestStore_ListNodes_AllFilters (FR-17.1)
   - Insert nodes with varied status/under/assignee/node_type/priority/labels
   - Query with each multi-value filter combination
   - Verify OR within field, AND across fields

3. TestStore_ConcurrentMutations
   - 2 goroutines mutating different nodes against one store
   - No deadlock; both succeed; race detector clean

4. TestStore_ConcurrentSchemaMigration
   - 2 goroutines both call schema migration
   - PG advisory lock single-flights; one runs, other waits then sees result
   - Skipped on providers without advisory lock support (pgbouncer transaction mode)

5. TestStore_TLSVerifyFull
   - sslmode=verify-full succeeds
   - sslmode=verify-ca, prefer, disable rejected by mtix connection-string validator
   - Skipped on Docker (loopback)

6. TestStore_StatementTimeout
   - Issue long query (SELECT pg_sleep beyond timeout)
   - Verify abort within configured timeout

7. TestStore_AuditLogTriggers
   - Insert via mutation; verify audit_log row inserted
   - Attempt UPDATE on audit_log row -> raises exception
   - Attempt DELETE on audit_log row -> raises exception

8. TestStore_AuditLogAtomicWithMutation
   - Chaos: simulate transaction abort at various points
   - Verify no committed mutation lacks an audit row
   - Verify no orphaned audit row without a mutation

9. TestStore_NodeTypeCanonicalization (MTIX-7, MTIX-12)
   - Insert via raw SQL with intentionally wrong node_type for the depth (e.g., depth 0 stored as story)
   - Export via the store; verify normalized to canonical (depth 0 -> epic)
   - Round-trip: export -> import -> export produces byte-identical result

10. TestStore_SQLInjectionResistance (MTIX-9.1 pattern)
    - For each filter (under, status, type, assignee, priority, labels)
    - Pass malicious payloads (DROP TABLE, OR 1=1, etc.)
    - Verify table still exists, no rows returned, no error escalation

INTERFACE WIDENING

PGStore interface in e2e/postgres/contract_test.go currently only has Ping/Close/Exec. Widen to mirror the relevant subset of internal/store.Store needed for the assertions above. Likely additions:
- CreateNode, GetNode, UpdateNode, DeleteNode
- ListNodes(filter, opts)
- Export, Import (for round-trip canonicalization test)
- Possibly: schema migration trigger (for the migration concurrency test)

Or import the real internal/store package once 14.1 lands and the circular-dependency concern from 14.9 is resolved.

ACCEPTANCE
1. All 10 contract tests have meaningful assertions matching their names.
2. All run against Docker provider when available (no longer skip on ErrPGStoreNotReady once 14.1 registers).
3. All test names align with their assertions (no misleading naming).
4. Tests pass against Docker provider in CI.
5. Provider-conditional tests (TLS, advisory lock) skip cleanly on incompatible providers.
6. Coverage on contract tests >= 90%.
7. golangci-lint clean.
8. Race detector clean (`go test -tags=e2e -race ./e2e/postgres/`).
nodes.acceptance: 1. 10 contract tests have real assertions matching names. 2. Pass against Docker in CI (no longer skip via ErrPGStoreNotReady). 3. Names align with assertions. 4. Provider-conditional tests skip cleanly. 5. >=90% coverage. 6. Lint clean. 7. Race-detector clean.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Local-first team sync for collaborative mtix
nodes.description: Replaces MTIX-14 BYO PG approach. Each team member's mtix CLI keeps a local SQLite as the source of truth. A BYO Postgres sync hub (Supabase, Neon, RDS, self-hosted) acts as the central event log. Mutations queue locally and flush to the hub asynchronously; reads pull on demand. Survives offline work, PG outages, agent surges. Aligns with mtix's local-first DNA. Setup-once-and-forget is a non-negotiable design property — no daemons required, no periodic maintenance, no babysitting.
nodes.prompt:
SCOPE

13 subtasks deliver collaborative mtix that meets the seven product properties: easy adoption, internet collaboration, zero data loss, no corruption, self-sustaining (no daemons or cron required), easy to configure, set up once and forget.

DELIVERABLES

15.0  Design lock-in: sync protocol, trust model, threat model, decision log, REQUIREMENTS.md FR-18
15.1  Sync event schema (local + hub), event recording on every mutation, queue durability
15.2  Hub transport (PushEvents/PullEvents API), TLS verify-full, DSN handling, retry/backoff
15.3  Event apply engine (idempotent replay, NodeTypeForDepth canonicalization carry-over)
15.4  Conflict detection (vector clocks) and resolution (LWW with surface)
15.5  Divergent-history detection and reconciliation (rename / import-as / discard-local)
15.6  CLI commands (sync init/clone/push/pull/status/daemon, conflicts list/resolve, doctor)
15.7  MCP integration (mtix_workflow rescoped for sync setup recommendations)
15.8  E2E integration tests (3 simulated CLIs + 1 hub, agent surge, lost laptop, queue full)
15.9  Performance benchmarks (sync push/pull, daemon overhead, memory)
15.10 Security audit pass 2 + sign-off (re-verify all design audit items in shipped code)
15.11 Documentation revision (SECURITY-MODEL.md, workflow docs, hook scripts, USERMANUAL)
15.12 v0.2.0-beta release prep

EXPLICITLY OUT OF SCOPE
- Hosted SaaS by HyperSWE (separate roadmap)
- Multi-tenant security model (BYO sync hub = trusted team only)
- CRDT-based conflict resolution (vector clock + LWW is sufficient for tasks)
- Schema migration tools beyond automatic on-connect (no offline migration)
- Per-user PG roles with RLS (advanced; defer to v2)

CROSS-CUTTING REQUIREMENTS (apply to every subtask)

Backwards compatibility:
- Existing v0.1.x SQLite users keep working unchanged
- Sync is opt-in via mtix sync init
- All existing tests pass with no changes (sync code is additive)

Test discipline:
- TDD: failing tests written first, then implementation
- Coverage: >= 90 percent on new code; 95 percent on critical paths (event apply, conflict resolution, reconciliation, schema migration)
- Total project Go coverage: >= 87.5 percent (no regression from current baseline)
- Race detector clean (every sync code path race-tested)
- No empty stub bodies; every test asserts real behavior matching its name

Determinism (carry-over from MTIX-9 audit):
- Map iteration that produces ordered output sorts deterministically
- Same event log -> same final state on every replay (property-based test)
- LWW resolution deterministic given timestamps + author hash

Atomic discipline (carry-over from MTIX-12 work):
- Every store mutation writes its sync event in the same transaction (chaos test confirms no orphaned events)
- NodeTypeForDepth canonicalization enforced on event apply (matches MTIX-7/MTIX-12)
- Audit completeness: every committed mutation has exactly one sync event

Security defaults (carry-over from MTIX-14 audit):
- TLS verify-full default; refuse weaker except --insecure-tls localhost
- DSN sources: MTIX_SYNC_DSN env var only OR .mtix/secrets file (gitignore-enforced, mode 0600); refuse tracked config
- DSN never appears in logs, error messages, or MCP tool output (regression test in every subtask that touches sync code)
- Schema migration via PG advisory lock (single-flight even with concurrent CLIs)

Operational discipline:
- No daemon required (sync triggers in-process on mutations; daemon mode is opt-in for real-time UX)
- No periodic maintenance (no "you must run X every week")
- Self-healing failure modes (transient errors retry with bounded backoff; permanent errors surface via mtix sync doctor)
- Idempotent operations (re-running any sync command is safe)

ACCEPTANCE
1. All 13 subtasks complete with their own acceptance criteria.
2. v0.1.x SQLite users see no change in behavior.
3. End-to-end: 3 simulated CLIs share one Docker PG, full lifecycle test passes (init, mutations, push, pull, conflict, reconcile, divergent history, recovery).
4. Cloud E2E: same suite passes against Supabase + Neon in release CI.
5. Performance budgets met: sync push of 1000 events under 5s, per-command latency unchanged from solo (<10ms).
6. Documentation revised: SECURITY-MODEL.md, workflow docs, hook scripts, USERMANUAL all reflect sync model.
7. Security audit pass 2 confirms all design audit items hold in shipped code.
8. Race detector clean across all sync code.
9. Total project coverage >= 87.5 percent.
10. v0.2.0-beta tagged and released; brew install works; clean install + sync init succeeds against real Supabase.
nodes.acceptance: 1. 13 subtasks complete. 2. SQLite backward compat. 3. E2E lifecycle (3 CLIs, 1 PG) green. 4. Cloud E2E (Supabase, Neon) green in release CI. 5. Performance budgets met. 6. Docs revised. 7. Security audit pass 2 signed off. 8. Race-detector clean. 9. Coverage >= 87.5%. 10. v0.2.0-beta released; brew + sync init validated.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Sync protocol design + threat model + decision log + REQUIREMENTS.md FR-18
nodes.description: Design lock-in. No code. Output: detailed sync protocol spec, trust model, threat model with mitigations, decision log (sync vs BYO PG vs TigerFS, vector clocks vs CRDTs, events vs diffs, Lamport vs wall-clock), mgit integration design note, REQUIREMENTS.md FR-18 added with all sub-requirements. Architecture diagram. Security audit reviewed and signed off. This blocks every other 15.x ticket.
nodes.prompt:
Design-only ticket. Produce these artifacts in this order — no code in this ticket.

1. PROTOCOL VERSIONING (NEW — addresses HIGH severity audit finding)
   - Define MTIX_SYNC_PROTOCOL_VERSION constant (start at 1).
   - Every PushEvents/PullEvents request includes client protocol version in metadata.
   - Hub rejects events from clients with incompatible major version with structured error code SYNC_VERSION_MISMATCH and a one-line upgrade hint.
   - Document major-vs-minor version policy: minor = additive, major = breaking, hub MUST refuse cross-major.
   - Document migration path: old client + new hub, new client + old hub, mixed-version teams.

2. PRIVACY/PII STATEMENT (NEW — MEDIUM)
   - Document explicitly: mtix does NOT classify or scrub PII from node titles, descriptions, prompts, comments, or any user-supplied text.
   - State that the hub stores whatever the team writes into the nodes, verbatim.
   - State explicitly: mtix is NOT GDPR/HIPAA/PCI-compliant; per-tenant deletion is best-effort via mtix delete (which writes a tombstone, not a true erasure).
   - Recommend teams that need GDPR right-to-erasure either use the planned hosted SaaS or rotate the entire hub DB.

3. SCALE LIMITS (NEW — MEDIUM)
   - Document tested-and-supported limits: <=10 active developers per hub, <=10K nodes per project, <=1M events per project lifetime.
   - Document what fails at scale: pull latency grows linearly with event count; full clone time > 30s past 100K events; UI pagination is required.
   - Hub event log retention defaults to forever; teams over the supported limit MUST configure compaction (deferred to v2 of schema, see 15.2).

4. SIDE-CHANNEL NON-APPLICABILITY (NEW — LOW)
   - Document that mtix is not a confidentiality boundary; all team members with the DSN see all data. Therefore timing-attack and side-channel mitigations are out of scope.
   - This is explicit so future contributors do not waste effort hardening against a non-threat.

5. CONFLICT STORM UX (NEW — MEDIUM)
   - Specify behavior when >50 conflicts accumulate: surface a banner in mtix sync status, group conflicts by node ID, support batch resolution (15.5 implements).
   - Specify what mtix does when an agent encounters a node that has unresolved conflicts: read returns the LWW-winning value but mtix context appends a CONFLICT block listing the alternate values; agents are instructed to escalate via mtix_comment rather than choose.

6. TIMESTAMP VALIDATION (NEW — HIGH)
   - Specify: every event has wall_clock_ts (int64 ms since epoch) AND lamport_clock (int64).
   - Hub MUST reject events with wall_clock_ts > now+24h with SYNC_TIMESTAMP_FUTURE error. This bounds clock-skew abuse for the LWW tie-break.
   - Hub MUST accept (but log a warning for) events with wall_clock_ts < now-30d (legitimate replay from a long-offline laptop is allowed).
   - Conflict resolution uses lamport_clock primarily, wall_clock_ts only as deterministic tie-break.

7. STOLEN DSN THREAT (NEW — HIGH)
   - Document explicitly in threat model: a stolen DSN gives full read+write to the hub. There is no per-user authentication beyond the DSN.
   - Recommended mitigation: rotate the DSN by rotating the PG role password; existing CLIs fail closed and re-prompt.
   - Future hardening (out of scope for v0.2): per-user PG roles + row-level security.

8. ORIGINAL SCOPE (preserved):
   - Detailed sync protocol spec (event format, push/pull endpoints, error codes).
   - Trust model (who is trusted, who is not).
   - Decision log: sync vs BYO PG vs TigerFS; vector clocks vs CRDTs; events vs diffs; Lamport vs wall-clock.
   - mgit integration design note (event bus contract).
   - REQUIREMENTS.md FR-18 added with all sub-requirements (FR-18.1 through FR-18.N).
   - Architecture diagram (ASCII or mermaid in the design doc).
   - Security audit reviewed and signed off in this ticket as a comment.

ACCEPTANCE: design doc exists at docs/SYNC-DESIGN.md, REQUIREMENTS.md FR-18 added, decision log present, all 7 NEW items above explicitly addressed and cross-referenced from the design doc. Sign-off comment posted on this ticket. NO CODE in this ticket — every other 15.x ticket is blocked on this one.

nodes.title: Performance benchmarks + load tests
nodes.description: Targets: solo mtix latency unchanged (<10ms per command); sync push of 1000 events <5s; sync pull of 1000 events <5s; daemon CPU <1% at idle; memory <50MB for 100K-node project; concurrent connections per CLI <=5. Benchmarks in benchmarks/sync_test.go; results emitted as CI artifacts. Targets met OR documented as known-limitations with planned mitigation.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Sync push/pull throughput benchmarks (1000 events <5s)
nodes.prompt:
Add benchmarks for the sync wire path. Gate on MTIX_PG_TEST_DSN; skip when unset.

Location: benchmarks/sync_throughput_test.go (new directory). Use the same harness primitives from e2e/sync_e2e_test.go — copy the fakeCLI helpers (NOT shared via import, since e2e is a test package) into a small benchmark-local test helper. Keep the duplication explicit and minimal.

Benchmarks:
- BenchmarkSyncPush_1000Events: seed 1000 create-node events on a fakeCLI, then time pushAll. Report ns/op AND elapsed time. Assert elapsed < 5s.
- BenchmarkSyncPull_1000Events: pre-populate hub with 1000 events (via CLI A's push), then time CLI B's pullAll. Assert elapsed < 5s.
- BenchmarkSyncPushPullRoundTrip_100Events: smaller round-trip benchmark for tighter feedback during dev iteration.

Each benchmark uses b.ResetTimer() after seeding so the timing measures only the operation under test.

Also add a regular test (TestPerf_PushPullTargets) that runs ONCE and asserts the 5s targets — Go benchmarks don't fail on time; we need an explicit assertion path so CI can gate on it.

Acceptance:
- Benchmarks run cleanly when MTIX_PG_TEST_DSN is set.
- TestPerf_PushPullTargets asserts <5s for both push and pull of 1000 events; if it fails, the failure message includes elapsed time so operators can see by how much.
- Document the targets in benchmarks/README.md (1-2 paragraphs) so future readers know the bar.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Solo CLI latency + memory + connection-pool checks
nodes.prompt:
Add three perf checks that DO NOT need PG.

**Solo CLI latency (<10ms per command):**
- BenchmarkSolo_Create_NoSyncOverhead: time store.CreateNode end-to-end (event emit included).
- BenchmarkSolo_Update_NoSyncOverhead: same for UpdateNode.
- BenchmarkSolo_List_NoSyncOverhead: time ListNodes on a 1000-node store.
- TestPerf_SoloCommandTargets: assert each operation's median time < 10ms. Use a 100-iteration loop and compute median (not mean — outliers from CI noise dominate the mean). Report median + p95 in the failure message.

**Memory at 100K nodes:**
- TestPerf_Memory_100KNodes: build a sqlite.Store, insert 100K nodes via CreateNode, force runtime.GC, then read runtime.MemStats.HeapAlloc and HeapInuse. Assert HeapAlloc < 50MB.
- Insertion is bulk and may take several seconds — that's fine, it's not the operation being measured. Use t.Skip if testing.Short() to keep regular runs fast.

**Connection-pool ceiling (<=5):**
- TestPerf_PoolMaxConnections: parse the transport.Pool's pgxpool.Config and assert MaxConns <=5. This is a static check — fast, no PG needed because we're inspecting the config, not connecting.

Location: extend benchmarks/ with solo_latency_test.go, memory_test.go, pool_config_test.go.

If any target is NOT met, mark it as a known-limitation in benchmarks/README.md WITH a planned mitigation (e.g. 'memory at 100K nodes: 65MB observed; mitigation: streaming export for backup, planned for v0.3').

Acceptance:
- All 3 latency benchmarks run.
- TestPerf_SoloCommandTargets passes OR documents known-limitations with mitigation.
- TestPerf_Memory_100KNodes passes OR documents known-limitations with mitigation.
- TestPerf_PoolMaxConnections passes (this should be unconditional — we control the pool config).
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Security audit pass 2 + sign-off
nodes.description: Re-run all 12 audit items from MTIX-15.0 design audit against shipped code. For each: confirm test exists and passes; verify behavior matches design. Penetration-style checks: malformed events rejected, replay attacks idempotent, LWW manipulation deterministic, DSN never in any log/error/MCP-output (regression test sweeps), TLS verify-full enforced (regression test), audit_log atomic with mutations (chaos test), node_type canonicalization at apply (regression for MTIX-7/12). Map-iteration determinism check (carry-over from MTIX-9 audit). Sign-off documented in this ticket comment.
nodes.prompt:
Re-run the full security audit against the SHIPPED code from MTIX-15.2 through 15.10. For each item, confirm a test exists and passes; verify the runtime behavior matches the design.

AUDIT MATRIX (12 items from MTIX-15.1 design audit):
1. Malformed events rejected (per 15.3 schema validator).
2. Replay attacks idempotent (per 15.4 applied_events dedupe).
3. LWW manipulation deterministic (per 15.5 lamport + author hash tie-break).
4. DSN never appears in any log, error message, MCP output, or panic - run a regression sweep that injects a synthetic DSN and greps the entire stderr/stdout/log output.
5. TLS verify-full enforced (per 15.3 TLS test).
6. audit_log atomic with mutations (chaos test from 15.2).
7. node_type canonicalization at apply (15.4 regression).
8. Map iteration determinism (carry-over from MTIX-9 audit; fail any test that depends on map-iteration order).
9. Schema migration single-flight via PG advisory lock (15.3 concurrent test).
10. SQL injection regression (15.3 port of MTIX-9.1 patterns).
11. Singleton pusher under agent surge (15.2 chaos test with 9 concurrent agents).
12. Reconciliation atomicity (15.6 failure injection tests).

PENETRATION-STYLE CHECKS:
- Run a fuzzer (see NEW item 1) against the transport.
- Attempt to bypass LWW by submitting events with manipulated lamport_clock - verify the hub rejects.
- Attempt to overflow vector_clock entries - verify rejection (NEW item 2).
- Run mtix with -trimpath and verify no DSN-shaped strings appear in built binary's strings output for a non-DSN test config.

NEW REQUIREMENTS FROM SECURITY/SAFETY REVIEW:
1. FUZZ TESTING FOR TRANSPORT (HIGH) - Add Go native fuzz targets:
   - FuzzEventDecode: feed random bytes into the event JSON decoder; never panic.
   - FuzzVectorClockMerge: feed random pairs of vector clocks; the merge result MUST equal merge(b,a) for all inputs (commutativity).
   - FuzzPushEventsValidation: feed random Event structs; the validator MUST either accept (and reject any later malformed mutation) or reject with a structured error - never panic, never partially apply.
   - Run for at least 10 minutes in CI (-fuzztime=10m on a dedicated CI job).
2. VECTOR CLOCK OVERFLOW TEST (HIGH) - Construct an event with vector_clock entries at MAX_INT64-1 and increment. Expected: structured error MTIX_SYNC_VC_OVERFLOW (per 15.3 schema validator's <2^53 check, leaving headroom). Test the boundary at exactly 2^53 and 2^53+1.
3. PANIC MESSAGE SANITIZATION SWEEP (HIGH) - Wrap the top-level main() and all goroutines with a defer-recover that runs the recovered value through redactDSN() (extend RedactDSN from MTIX-14.9 secret_redactor.go to all DSN-shaped patterns including jdbc:postgresql:// variants). Test: deliberately panic in a code path with a real DSN in scope; verify the printed panic does NOT contain the DSN.
4. AUDIT TRAIL FOR THE AUDIT - This ticket's sign-off comment must list, for each of the 12 items + 3 new items, the test file path + test name that proves it. Future auditors (human or LLM) can re-run the named tests without re-deriving the proof.

SIGN-OFF:
- Post a comment on this ticket with the audit results table (status: PASS/FAIL/SKIP per item) and the audited git SHA.
- If any item is FAIL or SKIP, file blocking sub-tickets and do NOT proceed to 15.12 release prep.

ACCEPTANCE:
- All 12 original audit items have a passing test + comment evidence.
- 3 new items (fuzz, vector clock overflow, panic sanitization) have passing tests + comment evidence.
- Sign-off comment posted with git SHA.
- govulncheck and CodeQL clean against the audited SHA.

Walk the 12 design-audit items from 15.0 (per the 15.11 description) and find the existing test that proves each. Build a structured evidence table: item → test file:line → assertion summary → PASS/FAIL/MISSING.

nodes.title: 3 new security requirements: fuzz + VC overflow + panic sanitization
nodes.prompt:
Implement the three NEW security requirements from the 15.11 prompt. Plus close any MISSING gaps from 15.11.1's audit.

**1. Fuzz targets for transport (Go native fuzz, gated by -fuzz):**
- FuzzEventDecode (in internal/sync/validator/ or internal/store/postgres/transport/): feed random bytes into the event JSON decoder; assert never panic (Go's fuzz harness does this automatically — any panic fails the corpus).
- FuzzVectorClockMerge (in internal/model/): feed two random vector clocks; assert merge(a,b) equals merge(b,a) (commutativity).
- FuzzPushEventsValidation (in internal/sync/validator/): feed random Event JSON; assert validator either accepts cleanly or rejects with a structured error — no panic, no partial.
- Each fuzz target must have a seed corpus (5-10 hand-crafted edge cases). The targets compile under default `go test`; only `-fuzz=Fuzz...` actually fuzzes.
- Document in docs/audit/MTIX-15-audit-pass2.md how to run them in CI: `go test -fuzz=FuzzEventDecode -fuzztime=10m ./internal/sync/validator/...`.

**2. Vector clock overflow test (regular test, runs every CI):**
- TestVectorClockOverflow_BoundaryAtMaxSafeInt: place in internal/sync/validator/ (where the <2^53 cap is enforced).
- Construct VC with one entry at exactly 2^53 (MaxVectorClockEntryValue per FR-18.7 / sync_clock.go). Validate. Expected: structured error (ErrInvalidInput-wrapped).
- Construct VC with one entry at 2^53 - 1 (boundary safe). Validate. Expected: PASS.
- Construct VC with one entry at 2^53 + 1 (overflow). Validate. Expected: structured error.
- Run pool.PushEvents with a synthetic event carrying VC at 2^53; assert it rejects pre-flight via the validator (does NOT reach PG).

**3. Panic message sanitization (HIGH):**
- redact.Recover already exists per MTIX-15.3.4. Verify it strips DSN-shaped strings from a panic value across postgres://, postgresql://, jdbc:postgresql:// schemes.
- TestRecover_StripsDSNFromPanicValue: trigger a panic with a real-looking DSN in the message; capture the recovered value; assert sentinel absent.
- TestRecover_StripsDSNFromPanicError: same but the panic value is an error wrapping a DSN.
- TestRecover_AllSchemes: postgres://, postgresql://, jdbc:postgresql://.
- Audit one main-loop entry point in cmd/mtix to confirm Recover() is wired in (or wire it in if it's not — that's the load-bearing change).

**Plus close any MISSING items from 15.11.1's audit.**

Acceptance:
- 3 fuzz targets compile + have seed corpora + are runnable.
- VC overflow test passes (3 boundary cases).
- Panic sanitization test passes (3 schemes + error-typed panics).
- Any MISSING audit items from 15.11.1 now have passing tests.
- go test -race -count=1 ./... green; golangci-lint 0 issues.

nodes.title: SECURITY-MODEL.md sync trust model rewrite
nodes.prompt:
Revise docs/SECURITY-MODEL.md to cover the FR-18 BYO Postgres sync hub trust model. The existing doc was written for a different storage architecture; rewrite about 30 percent.

Decisions to document:
- DSN handling: env var > .mtix/secrets (mode 0600) > NEVER tracked yaml. Refer to redact.DSN and redact.Recover (cmd/mtix/main.go wraps with defer Recover).
- TLS posture: verify-full is the default; weaker sslmode allowed only on loopback (transport.EnforceTLSPosture).
- Hub trust boundary: the hub authenticates writes; validator (internal/sync/validator) enforces schema/size/depth/lamport caps before any PG round-trip.
- Audit trail: hub triggers prevent UPDATE/DELETE on sync_conflicts/audit_log; pg_advisory_xact_lock single-flights migrations.
- LWW determinism: (lamport, wall_clock_ts, author_machine_hash) deterministic tiebreak ensures byzantine replicas converge.
- Document the VC-collision audit finding (from project_mtix15_audit_deferred.md): authorID-keyed VCs mean hub sync_conflicts is bypassed when CLIs share authorIDFallback='cli'; LWW at apply time still converges replicas; audit-trail visibility for same-authorID conflicts is reduced. Documents the design choice so future maintainers don't 'fix' it.
- Lost-laptop recovery: un-pushed events are NOT durable across machine loss; surfaced by mtix sync status pending count and the hub-unreachable detector in MTIX-15.8.
- Queue-full handling: ErrSyncQueueFull is surfaced; emit refuses rather than silently dropping.

Do NOT rewrite the whole file — preserve existing sections that are still accurate (the threat model framing, the SQLite local-store invariants). Insert a new top section 'Sync hub trust model (FR-18)' with the above content. Update any references to 'BYO Postgres as canonical store' (which was the v1.0 framing) — sync hub is NOT the canonical store; the local SQLite is. The hub is a replication mechanism.

Acceptance:
- docs/SECURITY-MODEL.md updated.
- VC-collision tradeoff explicitly documented (per project_mtix15_audit_deferred memory).
- 'BYO Postgres as canonical store' framing removed wherever it appears.
- markdown lints clean (no broken links).

nodes.title: Sync event schema + recording + queue durability
nodes.description: New tables: sync_events (local + hub), sync_conflicts (hub), sync_projects (hub). Migrations applied via existing schema framework + PG advisory lock for hub. Event recording in same transaction as data write (chaos test for atomicity). Local sync_status field tracks pending|pushed|conflicted. Queue durability via SQLite WAL. Configurable max queue size (default unlimited); refuse-new-on-full with clear error. Singleton pusher via .mtix/sync.push.lock file lock so 9 concurrent agents on one machine do not stampede the hub. New sentinel meta.sync.last_pulled_clock for local-vs-hub sync state (coexists with existing sentinels).
nodes.prompt:
Implement sync event recording + queue durability. Build on the design from MTIX-15.1 — refer to docs/SYNC-DESIGN.md for the canonical event schema.

SCHEMA:
- sync_events (local AND hub mirror): event_id (UUID v7), project_prefix, node_id, op_type (enum), payload (JSONB), wall_clock_ts (int64 ms), lamport_clock (int64), vector_clock (JSONB map), author_id, author_machine_hash, sync_status (local only: pending|pushed|conflicted|applied), created_at.
- sync_conflicts (hub only): conflict_id, event_id_a, event_id_b, node_id, field_name, resolution (lww|tombstone|manual), resolved_at, resolved_by.
- sync_projects (hub only): project_prefix PK, first_event_hash, created_at, schema_version.
- Migrations applied via existing schema framework + PG advisory lock single-flight (hub).
- New sentinel meta.sync.last_pulled_clock (int64) — coexists with existing tasks.json sentinels, never written by hand.

EVENT RECORDING:
- Every mutation that currently writes a node row MUST also write a sync_events row in the SAME local transaction (Atomicity test: kill -9 between node write and event write — neither persists OR both persist; chaos test required).
- Lamport clock = max(local_lamport, max(observed_lamport_from_pulled_events)) + 1.
- Vector clock entry for the local author_id incremented on every emit.

QUEUE DURABILITY:
- SQLite WAL + busy_timeout already provides durability; no additional layer.
- Configurable max queue size: setting sync.max_queue_size, default unlimited (0 = unlimited).
- When queue is full: refuse new mutations with structured error MTIX_SYNC_QUEUE_FULL plus a one-line guide ('mtix sync push --force or set sync.max_queue_size=0').

SINGLETON PUSHER:
- Background pusher acquires .mtix/sync.push.lock via flock (LOCK_EX|LOCK_NB).
- 9 concurrent agents on one machine: only one becomes pusher; others write events and exit immediately.
- Lock is auto-released on process exit (kernel-level guarantee, no manual cleanup needed).

NEW REQUIREMENTS FROM SECURITY/SAFETY REVIEW:
1. EVENT LOG RETENTION POLICY (MEDIUM) — Document in code AND in docs/SYNC-DESIGN.md: hub event log grows unbounded by default. Provide a setting hub.events_retention_days (default 0 = forever). Pruning compacts events older than N days into a single 'snapshot' event per node. THIS POLICY ITSELF IS DEFERRED TO v2 of the schema — but the schema column events.retained_until and the setting key MUST exist now so v2 is non-breaking. Include a TODO comment with rationale + ticket link.
2. COMPACTION DEFERRED WITH WRITTEN RATIONALE — Add a section in docs/SYNC-DESIGN.md titled 'Why compaction is deferred to v2' explaining: (a) v1 ships with a known unbounded-growth bound (1M events per project per design limit); (b) compaction requires consensus across all CLIs about which events were folded; (c) implementing it now would block beta on a non-blocking concern. Reference the supported scale limits from MTIX-15.1.

ACCEPTANCE:
- All migrations apply cleanly under PG advisory lock (concurrent-migration test passes).
- Event-write atomicity chaos test passes (kill -9 mid-mutation leaves no orphan events).
- Singleton pusher test passes (10 concurrent processes, only one acquires the lock).
- meta.sync.last_pulled_clock sentinel readable via mtix verify and matches sync_events MAX(lamport_clock).
- 90% line coverage on new code, race-detector clean.
- New schema columns (retained_until, retention setting) present with TODO comments referencing 15.2 and the v2 ticket placeholder.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Sync event model + helpers (UUID v7, Lamport, vector clock, machine hash)
nodes.description: Pure code: replace internal/model/sync_event.go with the FR-18.6 shape and add helper package internal/sync/clock for Lamport, vector clock, UUID v7, and machine_hash. No store integration in this ticket — just the types and helpers with unit tests.
nodes.prompt:
TDD: tests first, then implementation.

REPLACE internal/model/sync_event.go (existing struct is scaffolding, no production callers — confirmed by grep). New struct per SYNC-DESIGN.md §3.1 / FR-18.6:

type SyncEvent struct {
    EventID            string                 // UUID v7, primary key
    ProjectPrefix      string                 // e.g. 'MTIX'
    NodeID             string
    OpType             SyncOpType             // 12-value enum below
    Payload            json.RawMessage        // op-specific, validated against schema in 15.3
    WallClockTs        int64                  // ms since epoch
    LamportClock       int64
    VectorClock        VectorClock            // map[string]int64
    AuthorID           string
    AuthorMachineHash  string
    SyncStatus         SyncStatus             // pending|pushed|conflicted|applied (local mirror only)
    CreatedAt          time.Time
    RetainedUntil      *time.Time             // reserved for v2 compaction (FR-18.26); nil in v1
}

OpType enum (12 values, names match SYNC-DESIGN §3.3 exactly):
create_node, update_field, transition_status, claim, unclaim, defer, comment, link_dep, unlink_dep, delete, set_acceptance, set_prompt.

Payload structs (separate file internal/model/sync_payload.go, one per op_type):
- CreateNodePayload: full Node fields needed to recreate (title, parent_id, node_type, etc.)
- UpdateFieldPayload: {FieldName string, NewValue json.RawMessage, OldValue json.RawMessage}  (OldValue captured for conflict detection)
- TransitionStatusPayload: {From, To Status}
- ClaimPayload: {AgentID string, TTLSeconds int64}
- UnclaimPayload: {} (empty)
- DeferPayload: {Reason string, Until *time.Time}
- CommentPayload: {AuthorID, Body string}
- LinkDepPayload: {DependsOnNodeID, DepType string}
- UnlinkDepPayload: {DependsOnNodeID string}
- DeletePayload: {} (tombstone — empty)
- SetAcceptancePayload: {AcceptanceText string}
- SetPromptPayload: {PromptText string}

VectorClock helper type with methods: Bump(authorID), Merge(other), Dominates(other) bool, Concurrent(other) bool, MarshalJSON / UnmarshalJSON for stable serialization (sorted keys, deterministic — see FR-15.3a precedent).

NEW PACKAGE: internal/sync/clock with:
- NewEventID() string — UUID v7 via github.com/google/uuid v1.6.0 (NewV7 added in v1.5.0)
- MachineHash() string — first 16 hex chars of SHA-256 of: hostname + interface MAC (lowest non-loopback by name) + GOOS + GOARCH. Cached after first call. Stable across mtix invocations on same machine. NOT reset by reinstalling mtix.

NO database code in this ticket. NO emitter integration. JUST: types, payload structs, helpers, unit tests.

TESTS:
- TestSyncEvent_JSONRoundTrip: marshal then unmarshal each op_type, verify byte-identical.
- TestVectorClock_Bump: incrementing same author monotonic.
- TestVectorClock_Merge: commutative — merge(a,b) == merge(b,a) for arbitrary inputs (table-driven).
- TestVectorClock_DominatesAndConcurrent: matrix covering all 4 cases.
- TestVectorClock_MarshalJSON_DeterministicOrder: same map, marshal twice, byte-identical.
- TestNewEventID_FormatAndOrdering: UUID v7 format; consecutive IDs sort lexically by emission time.
- TestMachineHash_StabilityAndFormat: same value across N calls; matches /^[a-f0-9]{16}$/.
- TestOpType_StringRoundTrip: every op_type ↔ string is exact.

ACCEPTANCE:
- All tests pass.
- go vet, golangci-lint clean.
- 95% line coverage on internal/model/sync_event.go, internal/model/sync_payload.go, internal/sync/clock/*.go.
- No production code outside internal/model/* and internal/sync/clock/*.
nodes.acceptance: model.SyncEvent has FR-18.6 shape; 12 op_types defined; payload structs cover all 12; clock helpers (UUID v7, machine_hash, vector clock) implemented; 95% test coverage; race-detector clean.

nodes.title: Schema v2 migration: replace local sync_events + add applied_events + sync sentinels
nodes.description: Migrate the local SQLite schema from v1 (placeholder sync_events) to v2 (FR-18 sync_events + applied_events + meta.sync.* sentinels). No production data exists in old sync_events (confirmed by grep), so migration is a clean drop+create.
nodes.prompt:
TDD: tests first, then implementation.

CHANGES TO internal/store/sqlite/schema.go:
1. Bump schemaVersion to 2.
2. DROP TABLE IF EXISTS sync_events (old shape) inside the migration step.
3. CREATE TABLE sync_events with full FR-18.6 columns:
     event_id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
     project_prefix     TEXT NOT NULL,
     node_id            TEXT NOT NULL,
     op_type            TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(op_type IN (...12 values...)),
     payload            TEXT NOT NULL,            -- JSON
     wall_clock_ts      INTEGER NOT NULL,
     lamport_clock      INTEGER NOT NULL,
     vector_clock       TEXT NOT NULL,            -- JSON
     author_id          TEXT NOT NULL,
     author_machine_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
     sync_status        TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending' CHECK(sync_status IN ('pending','pushed','conflicted','applied')),
     created_at         TEXT NOT NULL,
     retained_until     TEXT,                      -- TODO(MTIX-v2): compaction reservation per FR-18.26
     FOREIGN KEY(node_id) REFERENCES nodes(id) ON DELETE NO ACTION
   Indexes:
     idx_sync_events_status_lamport ON sync_events(sync_status, lamport_clock) WHERE sync_status='pending'
     idx_sync_events_node ON sync_events(node_id)
     idx_sync_events_lamport ON sync_events(lamport_clock)
4. CREATE TABLE applied_events (per FR-18.9 idempotent dedupe):
     event_id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
     applied_at         TEXT NOT NULL,
     applied_by_lamport INTEGER NOT NULL
5. ADD meta keys at first init:
     INSERT OR IGNORE INTO meta (key, value) VALUES ('meta.sync.lamport', '0');
     INSERT OR IGNORE INTO meta (key, value) VALUES ('meta.sync.last_pulled_clock', '0');
     INSERT OR IGNORE INTO meta (key, value) VALUES ('hub.events_retention_days', '0');  -- TODO(MTIX-v2): compaction
     INSERT OR IGNORE INTO meta (key, value) VALUES ('sync.max_queue_size', '0');        -- 0 = unlimited per FR-18 / 15.2.4
     INSERT OR IGNORE INTO meta (key, value) VALUES ('meta.sync.machine_hash', '');      -- written on first emit by 15.2.3 helper
6. Migration logic: if SELECT value FROM meta WHERE key='schema_version' returns '1', execute step 2-5 in a single transaction. Bump schema_version to '2'. If meta.schema_version is missing or '2', no-op (fresh DB or already v2).
7. New SQL must be in the same monolithic schemaSQL const for fresh installs (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS friendly).

INSTRUMENTATION: emit a single INFO log on migration: schema_migrated event=schema_migrated from_version=1 to_version=2 dropped_tables=sync_events_old created_tables=sync_events,applied_events.

NO event-emitter wiring in this ticket. NO mutation changes. Just schema.

TESTS:
- TestSchema_FreshDBIsV2: open new DB, assert meta.schema_version='2', sync_events has new columns, applied_events exists, sentinels populated.
- TestSchema_V1ToV2Migration: simulate v1 by manually creating an old-shape sync_events + meta.schema_version='1', then re-open the store, assert migration ran, old data dropped (no rows preserved — old sync_events was scaffolding), new shape in place.
- TestSchema_MigrationIdempotent: open v2 DB, re-open it, assert migration code did NOT run again (use a counter or log capture).
- TestSchema_AllTablesExist: existing test (store_test.go:114) still passes — sync_events name unchanged.
- TestMeta_SyncSentinels: each new meta key readable, default values correct.
- TestSchema_SyncEvents_OpTypeCheck: try INSERT with op_type='invalid', expect CHECK constraint violation.
- TestSchema_SyncEvents_StatusCheck: same for sync_status.

ACCEPTANCE:
- All tests pass; existing tests unchanged.
- 95% line coverage on schema.go changes (the migration block).
- No regression in TestSchema_AllTablesExist.
- meta.sync.* sentinels visible via mtix verify (manual smoke).

DEPENDS ON: MTIX-15.2.1 — needs OpType enum string values to enforce CHECK constraints in CREATE TABLE.

nodes.title: Event emitter wired into every mutation (in same tx) + atomicity chaos test
nodes.description: Wire event emission into every store mutation. Each mutation method MUST insert one or more sync_events rows in the SAME tx as the node mutation. Plus the kill-9 chaos test that proves atomicity.
nodes.prompt:
TDD: tests first, then implementation.

NEW FILE internal/store/sqlite/sync_emit.go with:
- emitEvent(ctx, tx, event) — inserts the row; called from inside every mutation's WithTx.
- nextLamport(ctx, tx) int64 — atomically reads meta.sync.lamport, increments, writes back, returns new value. Uses SELECT ... FOR UPDATE-equivalent (SQLite SERIALIZABLE inside a tx is sufficient).
- currentVectorClock(ctx, tx) VectorClock — reads stored VC from meta key meta.sync.vector_clock (JSON map); empty map if absent.
- bumpAndPersistVC(ctx, tx, authorID) VectorClock — bumps local entry, writes back, returns new VC.
- machineHash(ctx, tx) string — reads/writes meta.sync.machine_hash; first call computes via internal/sync/clock.MachineHash() and persists.
- authorID(ctx) string — reads from context (set by command layer; falls back to 'cli' for tests).
- projectPrefix(ctx, tx, nodeID) string — derives from node_id.
- buildEvent(...) builds the SyncEvent ready for insertion.

WIRING (one mutation at a time, with a test for each):
| Mutation file:func | OpType | Payload |
| node_create.go:CreateNode | create_node | full Node serialization |
| node_update.go:UpdateNode | update_field, set_acceptance, set_prompt | one event per field changed; use set_acceptance for acceptance, set_prompt for prompt, update_field for everything else |
| transition.go:TransitionStatus | transition_status | {from, to} |
| claim.go:ClaimNode | claim | {agent_id, ttl_seconds} |
| claim.go:UnclaimNode | unclaim | {} |
| claim.go:ForceReclaimNode | claim | {agent_id, ttl_seconds, forced=true} |
| node_delete.go:DeleteNode | delete | {} (tombstone) |
| node_delete.go:UndeleteNode | NO EVENT — restore is idempotent against tombstone; document as local-only operation in comment |
| cancel.go:CancelNode | transition_status | {from, to=cancelled, reason} |
| dependency.go:AddDependency | link_dep | {depends_on, dep_type} |
| dependency.go:RemoveDependency | unlink_dep | {depends_on} |
| context.go:SetAnnotations | comment | {author_id, body} — NOTE: SetAnnotations currently does NOT use WithTx. WRAP it in WithTx in this ticket so the event is atomic with the annotation write. |
| store.go:UpdateProgress | NO EVENT — derived field; document. |

UndeleteNode + UpdateProgress + SetAnnotations: each gets a comment in code explaining the choice.

CHAOS TEST (TestEmit_AtomicityUnderKill):
- Spawn child process via os/exec that opens the DB, calls a mutation (CreateNode), and SIGSTOPs itself between the node insert and the event insert via a hook (use a build-tag chaostest variant of emitEvent that calls os.Exit(137) before the INSERT INTO sync_events when MTIX_CHAOS_KILL=before_event_insert is set).
- Parent SIGKILLs the child after a 100ms delay.
- Parent re-opens the DB, asserts: either both nodes row AND sync_events row exist (mutation completed before kill) OR neither (mutation rolled back via tx). Never one without the other.
- Parameterize the kill point: between node_insert and event_insert, between event_insert and tx commit. Both classes of kill produce no orphan rows.
- Run the test 50 iterations to catch timing-sensitive bugs.

PER-MUTATION TESTS:
For each of the 12 wired mutations, write a test that:
1. Performs the mutation.
2. Reads back the most recent sync_events row.
3. Asserts: op_type matches, payload deserializes to expected shape, lamport_clock > 0 and monotonically increasing, vector_clock contains author_id, author_machine_hash matches the value stored in meta, project_prefix matches node's project, sync_status='pending', wall_clock_ts is within 5s of now.

UndeleteNode + UpdateProgress: explicit tests asserting NO sync_events row is added.

ACCEPTANCE:
- All 12 op_types emitted by their corresponding mutation.
- Atomicity chaos test passes 50 iterations clean.
- Lamport monotonicity test (sequential mutations) passes.
- Vector clock per-author increment test passes.
- 90% line coverage on sync_emit.go and the wired mutations.
- Race detector clean.

DEPENDS ON: MTIX-15.2.1 (model + helpers), MTIX-15.2.2 (schema).
nodes.acceptance: Every mutation emits the right op_type in the same tx; atomicity chaos test green over 50 iterations; UndeleteNode+UpdateProgress documented as no-event; 90% coverage on emit + wired mutations.

nodes.title: Queue size enforcement (sync.max_queue_size) + singleton pusher flock primitive
nodes.description: Two operational primitives: (1) refuse mutations when the local pending event queue exceeds the configured max; (2) flock-based singleton pusher lock so 10 concurrent agents on one machine produce exactly one pusher.
nodes.prompt:
TDD: tests first, then implementation.

PART A — QUEUE SIZE ENFORCEMENT:
- Read sync.max_queue_size from meta (set in 15.2.2; default '0' = unlimited).
- New helper internal/store/sqlite/sync_emit.go:enforceQueueLimit(ctx, tx) error called BEFORE emitEvent in every wired mutation.
- If max > 0 and pending count >= max, return MTIX_SYNC_QUEUE_FULL structured error: 'mtix sync queue is full (N events pending; configured limit M). Run: mtix sync push --force, or raise the limit: mtix config set sync.max_queue_size 0'.
- Pending count = SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sync_events WHERE sync_status='pending'.
- New error type model.ErrSyncQueueFull (sentinel) so callers can errors.Is.

CONFIGURATION:
- New CLI subcommand: mtix config set sync.max_queue_size <N> (writes to meta).
- New CLI subcommand: mtix config get sync.max_queue_size (reads from meta).
- These reuse any existing config-set/config-get plumbing if it exists; otherwise a minimal cobra command in cmd/mtix/config.go.

PART B — SINGLETON PUSHER FLOCK:
- New file internal/sync/pushlock/pushlock_unix.go (build constraint: !windows).
- New file internal/sync/pushlock/pushlock_windows.go (build constraint: windows).
- Function: AcquirePushLock(mtixDir string) (*PushLock, error) — opens .mtix/data/sync.push.lock with O_CREATE|O_RDWR mode 0600; calls syscall.Flock(LOCK_EX|LOCK_NB) on Unix; LockFileEx on Windows.
- On contention: returns ErrPushLockHeld sentinel.
- Method: (l *PushLock) Release() error — Flock(LOCK_UN) + Close on Unix; UnlockFileEx + Close on Windows.
- The lock is auto-released on process exit (kernel guarantee). Release() is for graceful explicit cases.
- Mirror — do NOT import — internal/service/flock_*.go's pattern. Putting the lock under internal/sync/pushlock keeps the new sync tree self-contained.

NO actual pusher logic in this ticket. The pusher itself lands in MTIX-15.7. This ticket only provides the lock primitive.

TESTS:
PART A:
- TestQueueLimit_Unlimited (max=0): emit 100 events, all succeed.
- TestQueueLimit_Reached: set max=5, emit 5, the 6th mutation returns ErrSyncQueueFull with the documented message.
- TestQueueLimit_AfterPush: set max=5, emit 5, mark first 3 as pushed, emit 3 more (total 5 pending), succeed.
- TestQueueLimit_Reentry: same DB, two goroutines emit; both serialized by tx; neither sees stale count.
- TestConfig_SetGetMaxQueueSize: round-trip via the new CLI subcommands.

PART B:
- TestPushLock_SingleAcquire: one goroutine acquires; second concurrent acquire returns ErrPushLockHeld.
- TestPushLock_AfterRelease: acquire, release, second acquire succeeds.
- TestPushLock_TenConcurrent: spawn 10 goroutines; assert exactly one acquires; the other 9 receive ErrPushLockHeld; the holder releases; one of the others can then acquire.
- TestPushLock_AutoReleaseOnExit (subprocess test): child acquires the lock then os.Exit(0); parent immediately acquires it.
- Cross-platform: tests run on linux + macOS (CI matrix); windows test stubbed if CI does not run windows yet.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Queue limit enforced atomically with mutation.
- ErrSyncQueueFull message matches FR-18 exactly.
- Singleton lock test: 10 concurrent goroutines, exactly one wins.
- 90% coverage on pushlock + queue-limit code.
- Race-detector clean.

DEPENDS ON: MTIX-15.2.3 (uses emitEvent in tests; emitEvent must exist).
nodes.acceptance: max_queue_size honored with structured error; flock primitive correctly serializes 10 concurrent acquirers; cross-platform; 90% coverage; race-detector clean.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Hub schema SQL files (deferred execution) + supersede NFR-3 with FR-18 pointer
nodes.description: Two cleanup pieces: hub PG schema as SQL files (read by 15.3 transport, not executed in this ticket), and a documentation pass to mark NFR-3 (Cloud Sync) superseded by FR-18 in REQUIREMENTS.md so future contributors do not act on the old placeholder.
nodes.prompt:
Pure files + docs. No code wiring.

PART A — HUB SCHEMA SQL FILES:
Create internal/store/postgres/migrations/ with these files (read but NOT executed by this ticket):

001_sync_events.sql:
CREATE TABLE sync_events (
    event_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    project_prefix TEXT NOT NULL,
    node_id TEXT NOT NULL,
    op_type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (op_type IN ('create_node','update_field','transition_status','claim','unclaim','defer','comment','link_dep','unlink_dep','delete','set_acceptance','set_prompt')),
    payload JSONB NOT NULL,
    wall_clock_ts BIGINT NOT NULL,
    lamport_clock BIGINT NOT NULL,
    vector_clock JSONB NOT NULL,
    author_id TEXT NOT NULL,
    author_machine_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    retained_until TIMESTAMPTZ
);
CREATE INDEX idx_sync_events_project_lamport ON sync_events(project_prefix, lamport_clock);
CREATE INDEX idx_sync_events_node ON sync_events(node_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_sync_events_retained ON sync_events(retained_until) WHERE retained_until IS NOT NULL;

002_sync_conflicts.sql:
CREATE TABLE sync_conflicts (
    conflict_id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    event_id_a TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES sync_events(event_id),
    event_id_b TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES sync_events(event_id),
    node_id TEXT NOT NULL,
    field_name TEXT NOT NULL,
    resolution TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (resolution IN ('lww','tombstone','manual')),
    resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    resolved_by TEXT
);

003_sync_projects.sql:
CREATE TABLE sync_projects (
    project_prefix TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    first_event_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    schema_version INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    last_seen_cli_version TEXT
);

004_applied_events.sql:
CREATE TABLE applied_events (
    event_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    applied_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    applied_by_lamport BIGINT NOT NULL
);

005_audit_log.sql:
CREATE TABLE audit_log (
    audit_id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    project_prefix TEXT NOT NULL,
    actor TEXT NOT NULL,
    action TEXT NOT NULL,
    target_node_id TEXT,
    payload JSONB,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);
CREATE INDEX idx_audit_log_project_time ON audit_log(project_prefix, created_at);

006_triggers.sql (per FR-18.5 — append-only enforcement):
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION audit_log_immutable() RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
BEGIN
    RAISE EXCEPTION 'audit_log is append-only; UPDATE/DELETE forbidden (FR-18.5)';
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
CREATE TRIGGER audit_log_no_update BEFORE UPDATE ON audit_log FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION audit_log_immutable();
CREATE TRIGGER audit_log_no_delete BEFORE DELETE ON audit_log FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION audit_log_immutable();
CREATE TRIGGER sync_conflicts_no_update BEFORE UPDATE ON sync_conflicts FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION audit_log_immutable();
CREATE TRIGGER sync_conflicts_no_delete BEFORE DELETE ON sync_conflicts FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION audit_log_immutable();

-- Documentation only (used by 15.3 transport):
-- pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtext('mtix_sync_migration')) inside the migration tx.
-- This file is a placeholder; the actual lock acquisition happens in Go code in 15.3.

Each file MUST start with a comment block: -- MTIX-15.2 hub schema. Executed by MTIX-15.3 transport (PG advisory lock single-flight). DO NOT run manually.

nodes.title: Hub transport (PushEvents/PullEvents API + TLS + retry)
nodes.description: PG client wrapper exposing PushEvents(events) -> accepted_ids, conflicts; PullEvents(since_clock) -> events. pgxpool with sane defaults. Statement timeout + connection timeout. Exponential backoff for retries. TLS verify-full default; refuse weaker except --insecure-tls localhost. DSN sourcing: MTIX_SYNC_DSN env var only OR .mtix/secrets file; refuse tracked config. Schema migration on first connect with PG advisory lock. SQL injection regression test (port from MTIX-9.1 pattern).
nodes.prompt:
Implement the hub transport layer: PG client wrapper that exposes PushEvents and PullEvents over the connection pool. Build on MTIX-15.1 design and MTIX-15.2 schema.

API SHAPE:
- PushEvents(ctx, []Event) (accepted_event_ids []string, conflicts []ConflictDescriptor, err error)
- PullEvents(ctx, since_lamport int64, limit int) ([]Event, has_more bool, err error)
- Both go through pgxpool with sane defaults: max_conns=8, conn_lifetime=30m, statement_timeout=10s, healthcheck_period=1m.
- Exponential backoff on transient errors (network, statement_timeout): base=200ms, factor=2, max=10s, max_attempts=5. Permanent errors (auth, schema mismatch) fail immediately.

TLS DEFAULTS:
- sslmode=verify-full default. Refuse weaker (verify-ca, prefer, disable) UNLESS --insecure-tls flag set AND host resolves to loopback.
- SSLROOTCERT support (NEW MEDIUM): honor MTIX_SYNC_SSLROOTCERT env var and DSN sslrootcert= parameter. Document that managed PG providers (Supabase, Neon, RDS) often need this. Test with a self-signed cert chain.

DSN SOURCING:
- MTIX_SYNC_DSN env var only OR .mtix/secrets file (mode 0600, gitignored, validate at load time).
- Refuse to load DSN from any tracked YAML/JSON config file (fail-closed at config-load).
- Test: place DSN in .mtix/config.yaml, expect refusal with structured error MTIX_SYNC_DSN_IN_TRACKED_FILE.

SCHEMA MIGRATION ON FIRST CONNECT:
- Acquire PG advisory lock pg_advisory_xact_lock(hash('mtix_sync_migration')) inside the migration tx.
- 10 concurrent CLIs first-connect: only one runs the migration, others see the result.
- Concurrent-migration test from 15.2 must pass against this transport.

NEW REQUIREMENTS FROM SECURITY/SAFETY REVIEW:
1. SCHEMA VALIDATION WITH SIZE LIMITS (HIGH) - Validate every incoming event in PushEvents BEFORE applying:
   - event_id is valid UUID v7.
   - op_type is one of the 12 documented enum values.
   - payload is valid JSON, <=64KB serialized, no nested depth >10.
   - wall_clock_ts is int64, non-negative, <= now+24h (reject SYNC_TIMESTAMP_FUTURE per 15.1).
   - lamport_clock is int64, non-negative.
   - vector_clock is a flat map[string]int64, <=100 entries, each value <2^53 (overflow protection - also see 15.11).
   - author_id matches /^[a-z0-9_-]{1,64}/.
   - Reject malformed events with structured error MTIX_SYNC_EVENT_INVALID + which field failed. NEVER apply a partially-validated event.
2. REJECT FUTURE TIMESTAMPS (HIGH) - Hub-side check: if any event in batch has wall_clock_ts > server now+24h, reject ENTIRE batch with one error citing the offending event_id. Test with skew of +25h, +30d, +1y.
3. PARTIAL-MIGRATION RECOVERY TEST (MEDIUM) - Chaos test: kill the CLI process mid-migration (between two ALTER TABLE statements). Restart. Verify: advisory lock is released by PG (transaction rollback), next CLI re-runs the WHOLE migration cleanly, no half-applied schema. Use a hook in the test that pauses between statements and SIGKILLs the process.

SQL INJECTION REGRESSION:
- Port the MTIX-9.1 attack-pattern test to the PG transport.
- Every parameter passes through pgx bound-parameter API; string concatenation of user input into SQL is forbidden. Add a golangci lint rule (or a grep-based pre-commit) to enforce.

ACCEPTANCE:
- TLS verify-full enforced (test: connect with verify-ca fails closed).
- DSN refused from tracked config file (test passes).
- 10 concurrent first-connects: exactly one runs migration, advisory-lock observable in pg_locks.
- Schema validator rejects all 7 malformed-event classes with structured errors.
- Partial-migration kill-9 test passes.
- SQL injection regression passes.
- 90% coverage, race-detector clean.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: PG transport: pgxpool + DSN sourcing + TLS + schema migration + chaos test
nodes.description: Foundational PG connection layer for the hub transport. Establishes pgxpool with sane defaults, DSN sourcing (env or .mtix/secrets), TLS verify-full default, SSLROOTCERT support, and the schema migration runner that executes the 15.2.5 SQL files inside a single PG transaction guarded by pg_advisory_xact_lock.
nodes.prompt:
TDD: tests first, then implementation.

DEPENDENCIES:
- pgx v5 (github.com/jackc/pgx/v5) added as direct dependency.
- All PG-touching tests skip when env MTIX_PG_TEST_DSN is unset (so local laptops without PG and CI without PG service still pass).

NEW PACKAGE internal/store/postgres/transport with:

1. CONNECTION + POOL
- New(ctx, dsn, opts) (*Pool, error) — parses DSN, enforces TLS posture, opens pgxpool.
- Pool.Close() — closes the pool.
- Pool.HealthCheck(ctx) error — pings PG.
- Defaults: max_conns=8, conn_lifetime=30m, statement_timeout=10s, healthcheck_period=1m.

2. DSN SOURCING (Source function in same package)
- DSN MUST come from MTIX_SYNC_DSN env var OR .mtix/secrets file (mode 0600 enforced; gitignore check is best-effort).
- Refuse if .mtix/config.yaml or any tracked YAML/JSON contains 'sync.dsn'/'pg.dsn' key — return MTIX_SYNC_DSN_IN_TRACKED_FILE structured error.
- Return wrapped model.ErrInvalidInput with 'no DSN configured: set MTIX_SYNC_DSN or .mtix/secrets' if neither source provides one.

3. TLS POSTURE
- Default sslmode=verify-full when DSN omits sslmode.
- Refuse weaker (verify-ca, prefer, allow, disable) UNLESS opts.InsecureTLS=true AND host resolves to loopback (127.0.0.1, ::1, localhost).
- Honor MTIX_SYNC_SSLROOTCERT env var: if set, applies as sslrootcert= in DSN.

4. SCHEMA MIGRATION RUNNER
- Migrate(ctx) error — opens a tx, calls SELECT pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtext('mtix_sync_migration')), runs all migrations from internal/store/postgres/migrations.Files() in order via Read(). Commits.
- Idempotent: running Migrate twice on the same hub is a no-op (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS handles it).
- Single-flight: 10 concurrent Migrate calls produce exactly one runner; others wait for the advisory lock and observe the committed result.

TESTS (all skip if MTIX_PG_TEST_DSN unset):
- TestPool_OpenAndClose: open pool, healthcheck succeeds, close cleanly.
- TestPool_DefaultsApplied: connect, query SHOW statement_timeout, assert it's 10s.
- TestDSNSource_FromEnv: set env var, Source returns it.
- TestDSNSource_FromSecretsFile: write .mtix/secrets mode 0600, Source returns it.
- TestDSNSource_RefusesWrongMode: write .mtix/secrets mode 0644, Source returns mode-error.
- TestDSNSource_RefusesEmpty: no env, no secrets, Source returns wrapped ErrInvalidInput.
- TestDSNSource_RefusesTrackedConfig: write sync.dsn into .mtix/config.yaml, Source returns MTIX_SYNC_DSN_IN_TRACKED_FILE.
- TestTLS_DefaultsToVerifyFull: DSN without sslmode, parsed result has sslmode=verify-full.
- TestTLS_RefusesWeakerWithoutInsecureFlag: DSN with sslmode=disable, opts.InsecureTLS=false, refused.
- TestTLS_AllowsWeakerWithInsecureFlagOnLoopback: DSN with sslmode=disable, opts.InsecureTLS=true, host=127.0.0.1, accepted.
- TestTLS_RefusesWeakerOnRemoteHostEvenWithInsecureFlag: opts.InsecureTLS=true, host=db.example.com, sslmode=disable, refused.
- TestTLS_HonorsSSLROOTCERTEnv: env set, parsed DSN includes sslrootcert.
- TestMigrate_FreshHubAppliesAllSchema: empty PG, Migrate runs, all 7 SQL files apply, expected tables present.
- TestMigrate_Idempotent: run Migrate twice, no errors, schema still v1 (sync_projects.schema_version), trigger functions exist exactly once.
- TestMigrate_ConcurrentSingleFlight: 10 goroutines call Migrate, exactly one runs (assert via PG transaction count or by inserting a marker in the migration), rest wait, all see committed result.
- TestMigrate_AdvisoryLockReleasedOnCommit: after Migrate completes, pg_advisory_xact_lock has been released (next caller succeeds immediately).
- TestMigrate_PartialMigrationRecovery: chaos test — kill the goroutine mid-migration (between two Read+Exec pairs), verify next Migrate runs cleanly without leftover schema.

ACCEPTANCE:
- All tests pass (or skip if no test PG available).
- DSN never appears in any error message or log line emitted from this package.
- Schema migration single-flight tested with 10 concurrent calls.
- 90% coverage on the transport package (with PG tests running).

nodes.title: FR-18.7 event validator + Go native fuzz targets
nodes.description: Pure library: validates an event against the FR-18.7 schema (size limits, timestamp bounds, vector clock overflow, regex). Plus Go native fuzz targets per FR-18.27.
nodes.prompt:
TDD: tests first.

NEW FILE internal/store/postgres/transport/validator.go (or inline in transport package):
- Validate(*model.SyncEvent, now time.Time) error
- Wraps model.SyncEvent.Validate (which already enforces non-empty fields, regexes, sign).
- Adds the size and timestamp rules:
  - Payload <= 64*1024 bytes serialized -> MTIX_SYNC_PAYLOAD_TOO_LARGE
  - Payload JSON nesting depth <= 10 -> MTIX_SYNC_PAYLOAD_TOO_NESTED
  - WallClockTS <= now+24h -> MTIX_SYNC_TIMESTAMP_FUTURE
  - WallClockTS < now-30d -> log warning but accept
  - LamportClock < 2^53 -> MTIX_SYNC_LAMPORT_OVERFLOW (model already enforces this on VC entries; lamport gets the same cap here)
  - VectorClock.Validate() (model already enforces <=100 entries, each <2^53)
- ValidateBatch(events []*model.SyncEvent, now time.Time) error — first failing event aborts the whole batch with structured error wrapping.

NEW FUZZ TARGETS in internal/store/postgres/transport (fuzz_test.go):
- FuzzEventDecode: random bytes -> json.Unmarshal into model.SyncEvent. Must NEVER panic. Errors are fine.
- FuzzVectorClockMerge: random pairs of vector clocks; assert merge(a,b) == merge(b,a).
- FuzzPushEventsValidation: random Event constructions through Validate(). Must EITHER accept OR reject with structured error — never panic, never partially apply.

Configure CI to run go test -fuzz for at least 10 minutes on a dedicated job (this ticket only sets the targets up; CI wiring is a 15.10/15.13 concern).

UNIT TESTS:
- TestValidator_HappyPath
- TestValidator_RejectsOversizedPayload
- TestValidator_RejectsTooDeepPayload (construct nested JSON like {a:{a:{a...}}} 11 levels)
- TestValidator_RejectsFutureTimestamp_25h_30d_1y
- TestValidator_AcceptsBackwardsTimestamp_31d_with_warning
- TestValidator_RejectsLamportOverflow_at_and_above_2_53
- TestValidator_AcceptsLamport_just_below_2_53
- TestValidator_BatchAbortsOnFirstFailure
- TestFuzz seed corpus: at least 5 hand-crafted bad inputs each per fuzz target

ACCEPTANCE:
- All unit tests pass.
- Fuzz targets compile and run for 30s in the test loop without panic (10m in CI).
- 95% coverage on validator.go.

nodes.title: PushEvents + PullEvents API + retry/backoff
nodes.description: The hub-side push/pull endpoints. Validates events via 15.3.2, runs them in a tx via the 15.3.1 pool, returns accepted_ids and conflict descriptors.
nodes.prompt:
TDD: tests first.

API:
- type ConflictDescriptor struct { EventID, ConflictingEventID, NodeID, FieldName string }
- (p *Pool) PushEvents(ctx, events []*model.SyncEvent) (acceptedIDs []string, conflicts []ConflictDescriptor, err error)
- (p *Pool) PullEvents(ctx, sinceLamport int64, limit int) (events []*model.SyncEvent, hasMore bool, err error)

PUSH semantics:
- Validate the entire batch first (via 15.3.2 ValidateBatch); on any failure, reject the whole batch with structured error MTIX_SYNC_EVENT_INVALID and the failing event_id+field.
- INSERT all valid events into sync_events using a single tx with ON CONFLICT (event_id) DO NOTHING (idempotent re-push).
- Conflict detection: for each event, compare against existing events on (node_id, field_name) with overlapping vector clocks. If concurrent (per VectorClock.Concurrent), append a ConflictDescriptor. (Full LWW resolution is a 15.5 concern; this ticket just IDs conflicts.)
- Return accepted_ids = the events that landed (deduplicated against ON CONFLICT).

PULL semantics:
- SELECT * FROM sync_events WHERE lamport_clock > ? ORDER BY lamport_clock LIMIT ?+1.
- If returned count > limit, hasMore = true and trim.
- Return events in lamport order.

RETRY:
- Wrap PG calls in retryWithBackoff(maxAttempts=5, base=200ms, factor=2, max=10s).
- Retry only transient errors: pgx.ErrTxClosed, network errors, statement_timeout.
- Permanent errors (auth, schema mismatch, validation) fail immediately.

TESTS (skip if MTIX_PG_TEST_DSN unset):
- TestPushEvents_HappyPath: 5 valid events, all accepted.
- TestPushEvents_RejectsEntireBatchOnInvalid: 4 valid + 1 invalid, all rejected.
- TestPushEvents_IdempotentOnRepush: push 3, push same 3 again, accepted_ids on second push is empty.
- TestPushEvents_DetectsConcurrentEdits: push event A from author1, push event B from author2 with concurrent VC for same field, conflict descriptor returned.
- TestPullEvents_FromZero: pull from lamport=0, get all.
- TestPullEvents_FromHighWaterMark: push 10, pull from lamport=5, get 5.
- TestPullEvents_HasMoreFlag: push 20, pull with limit=10, hasMore=true.
- TestPullEvents_OrderedByLamport: push out-of-order events, pull, returned in lamport order.
- TestRetry_RetriesTransientThenSucceeds: inject transient error (pgx network), assert retry kicks in.
- TestRetry_FailsImmediatelyOnPermanent: inject auth error, assert no retry.
- TestRetry_BackoffBoundsRespected: simulate failures, assert delays ~ 200ms, 400ms, 800ms, 1600ms, 3200ms (with jitter tolerance).

ACCEPTANCE:
- PushEvents idempotent under repush.
- PullEvents respects lamport order and hasMore.
- Retry only on transient.
- Conflict descriptors returned (full resolution in 15.5).
- 90% coverage.

nodes.title: SQL injection regression + DSN hygiene sweep + panic-message redaction
nodes.description: Cross-cutting security: SQL injection regression test ported from MTIX-9.1; sweep that proves DSN never appears in any observable output; top-level defer-recover that runs panic values through RedactDSN.
nodes.prompt:
TDD: tests first.

PART A — SQL INJECTION REGRESSION:
- Port MTIX-9.1 attack patterns (e.g., ');DROP TABLE--, ' OR '1'='1) to PushEvents inputs.
- For each attack pattern, populate event fields (NodeID, AuthorID, Payload) with the pattern, push, verify:
  1. Either rejected by the validator (via regex), or
  2. Accepted as a literal string and stored verbatim with no SQL side-effect (e.g., target table still queryable).
- Test passes when no attack pattern causes data loss or unauthorized state change.

PART B — DSN HYGIENE SWEEP:
- New test TestDSN_NeverInObservableOutput: inject a synthetic DSN with a unique sentinel password ('PASSWORD_LEAK_SENTINEL_xyz123'), exercise the full transport surface (open pool, push events, pull events, force errors via bad SQL), and grep stdout, stderr, log buffer, MCP tool output for the sentinel string. Test fails if the sentinel appears anywhere.

PART C — PANIC-MESSAGE REDACTION:
- Extend internal/sync/clock or add internal/sync/redact package with RedactDSN(s string) string that masks postgres://, jdbc:postgresql://, and similar DSN-shaped substrings.
- Add a top-level defer-recover wrapper helper RecoverWithRedaction(logger) that callers can use in goroutine entry points. Logs the redacted panic value at ERROR.
- Test: deliberately panic with a real DSN in the panic value; verify the printed/logged output contains the redacted form, NOT the original DSN.

PART D — STATIC LINT FOR RAW SQL CONCAT:
- Add a pre-commit grep or golangci config rule that fails the build if any .go file under internal/store/postgres/ contains string concatenation into an SQL keyword (heuristic: '+' adjacent to 'SELECT'/'INSERT'/'UPDATE'/'DELETE'/'WHERE' inside a string).
- This is best-effort; the real protection is bound parameters, but this catches regressions early.

TESTS:
- TestSQLInjection_PortedFromMTIX91 (table-driven, 6+ patterns)
- TestDSN_NeverInLogs
- TestDSN_NeverInErrors
- TestDSN_NeverInPanic
- TestRedactDSN_HandlesAllSchemes (postgres://, postgresql://, jdbc:postgresql://, raw user:pass@host)
- TestRecoverWithRedaction_LogsRedacted

ACCEPTANCE:
- All SQL injection attack patterns either rejected or stored as literals.
- DSN sentinel grep returns zero matches across all observable outputs.
- Panic with DSN produces redacted output.
- 90% coverage on redact package.

DEPENDS ON: 15.3.1, 15.3.2, 15.3.3 (uses the full transport surface for the sweeps).
nodes.acceptance: SQL injection regression green; DSN sentinel never appears in any observable output; panic-message redaction works; lint for raw SQL concat in place.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Event apply engine (idempotent replay + node_type canonicalization)
nodes.description: IdempotentApply(event) function applies event to local nodes table within transaction. Reject duplicates (event_id seen). Update vector_clock on apply. Handle all 12 operation types (create, update_field, transition_status, claim, delete, etc.). On apply: enforce NodeTypeForDepth canonicalization (ignores any non-canonical node_type in the event payload, matches MTIX-7/12 contract). Tests: replay same event twice (idempotent); apply create-then-update sequence; apply out-of-order events with vector clock reordering; synthetic event with wrong node_type for depth applies canonical.
nodes.prompt:
Implement the IdempotentApply(event) function: take a single sync event and apply it to the local nodes table within a single SQLite transaction.

CONTRACT:
- IdempotentApply(ctx, tx, event) error
- Reject duplicate event_id silently (return nil, log debug). Detect via SELECT 1 FROM applied_events WHERE event_id=?.
- Update target node's vector_clock entry for event.author_id.
- Update local lamport clock = max(local, event.lamport_clock).
- Append to applied_events table (event_id, applied_at, applied_by_lamport).
- Handle all 12 op types from MTIX-15.1 design: create_node, update_field, transition_status, claim, unclaim, defer, comment, link_dep, unlink_dep, delete (tombstone), set_acceptance, set_prompt.

NODE_TYPE CANONICALIZATION (preserved from original):
- On every apply that touches node_type: ignore any non-canonical value in the event payload, ALWAYS use NodeTypeForDepth(node.depth). Matches MTIX-7/12 contract - a peer with a buggy local mtix cannot poison the hub's hierarchy.
- Test: synthetic event with depth=0 + node_type='story' applies as 'epic'.

ORIGINAL TESTS (preserved):
- Replay same event twice: idempotent, second is a no-op.
- Apply create-then-update sequence: final state matches direct mutation.
- Apply out-of-order events with vector clock reordering: state converges per LWW.
- Synthetic event with wrong node_type for depth applies canonical node_type.

NEW REQUIREMENTS FROM SECURITY/SAFETY REVIEW:
1. PROPERTY-BASED TEST FOR REPLAY DETERMINISM (HIGH) - Use github.com/leanovate/gopter or similar. Generate a random sequence of 100 events across 10 nodes. Apply in order A. Apply the same sequence in shuffled order B (where shuffling preserves vector-clock causal ordering). Final state MUST be byte-identical (compare nodes-table content_hash). Run with at least 1000 random seeds in CI.
2. CHAOS TEST FOR KILL-9 MID-APPLY (HIGH) - Test harness: spawn a child process that calls IdempotentApply on a known event, SIGKILL it after a configurable delay (parameterize 0ms to 50ms in 5ms steps). After kill, restart, verify either (a) the event is fully applied AND in applied_events, OR (b) the event is fully unapplied AND not in applied_events. Never partial. This proves the SQLite transaction boundary holds.
3. EVENT REPLAY AGAINST EMPTY DB (no event_id duplicate suppression bypass) - verify that an event with op_type=delete on a non-existent node is a no-op (does not create a phantom tombstone). Tombstones only mark deletion of previously-existing nodes.

ACCEPTANCE:
- All 12 op types covered with table-driven tests.
- Property test (1000 seeds) passes - final state determinism.
- Chaos test (10 kill points x 100 iterations) passes - atomicity.
- node_type canonicalization regression passes.
- 90% coverage on apply engine, race-detector clean.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Apply engine: IdempotentApply core + 12 per-op functions + NodeTypeForDepth canonicalization
nodes.description: Core event-apply engine. IdempotentApply takes one event, checks applied_events for duplicates, dispatches to a per-op_type apply function that writes directly to the nodes table (bypassing Store mutations to avoid emit loops), updates meta.sync.lamport and meta.sync.vector_clock, and records the event in applied_events. All 12 op_types covered.
nodes.prompt:
TDD: tests first.

NEW FILE internal/store/sqlite/sync_apply.go:
- IdempotentApply(ctx, tx, event) error — main entry point.
- Helpers per op_type: applyCreateNode, applyUpdateField, applyTransitionStatus, applyClaim, applyUnclaim, applyDefer, applyComment, applyLinkDep, applyUnlinkDep, applyDelete, applySetAcceptance, applySetPrompt.
- Helpers for clock/vc: advanceLamportTo(tx, observedLamport), mergeVectorClock(tx, observedVC).
- recordApplied(tx, event) — INSERT into applied_events.

CONTRACT:
- IdempotentApply MUST be called inside an open *sql.Tx (caller's responsibility).
- Reject duplicate event_id silently — return nil, log debug.
- DO NOT emit a sync_event (apply MUST NOT loop).
- For create_node: enforce NodeTypeForDepth(node.depth) — IGNORE any non-canonical node_type in the payload (FR-18.10 / MTIX-7/12 contract).
- For delete on non-existent node: no-op (no phantom tombstone).
- For update_field on non-existent node: error wrap model.ErrNotFound.
- After applying, ALWAYS bump local Lamport to max(local, event.lamport_clock) and merge VC entry for event.author_id.

12 OP_TYPES with corresponding payload struct + SQL UPDATE/INSERT/DELETE statement.

UNIT TESTS (per-op_type):
- TestApply_CreateNode_HappyPath
- TestApply_CreateNode_CanonicalizesNodeType (synthetic event with depth=0 + node_type='story' applies as 'epic')
- TestApply_UpdateField_Title (and a few representative fields)
- TestApply_UpdateField_OnMissingNode (returns ErrNotFound wrap)
- TestApply_TransitionStatus
- TestApply_Claim, Unclaim, ClaimWithForcedTrue
- TestApply_Delete (sets deleted_at)
- TestApply_Delete_OnNonExistentNode_Noop (no phantom tombstone)
- TestApply_LinkDep + UnlinkDep
- TestApply_Comment (appends to annotations)
- TestApply_SetAcceptance + SetPrompt
- TestApply_Idempotent (apply same event twice; second is no-op; applied_events has 1 row)
- TestApply_LamportAdvances (event.lamport=5 + local.lamport=2 -> local.lamport=5)
- TestApply_LamportNeverGoesBackwards (event.lamport=2 + local.lamport=5 -> local.lamport=5)
- TestApply_VectorClockMerges (per-author entry max-merged)
- TestApply_RejectsInvalidEvent (validator runs first; bad event surfaces ErrInvalidInput)
- TestApply_NoSyncEventEmitted (after apply, sync_events table is empty)

ACCEPTANCE:
- All 12 op_types apply correctly + idempotent.
- NodeTypeForDepth canonicalization regression test.
- No sync_event emitted by apply (verify by counting sync_events rows).
- 90% coverage on sync_apply.go.
nodes.acceptance: All 12 op_types apply via per-op functions; idempotent on duplicate; NodeTypeForDepth canonicalization preserved; no emit loop; 90% coverage.

nodes.title: Apply engine: replay determinism property test (1000 seeds)
nodes.description: Property-based test per FR-18.9 / SYNC-DESIGN section 8: generate random sequences of events; apply in order A; apply the same sequence shuffled (preserving causal order); assert final state byte-identical via content_hash. 1000 random seeds in CI.
nodes.prompt:
TDD: this ticket IS the test.

NEW FILE internal/store/sqlite/sync_apply_property_test.go:
- TestApply_ReplayDeterminismProperty(t *testing.T):
  - For each of N seeds (N=1000 in CI; N=50 in fast local runs via -short flag):
    1. Generate a random sequence of K events (K=100 in CI, K=20 in -short) across M nodes (M=10).
    2. Each event has a random op_type, payload, and lamport_clock that respects causal ordering (lamport monotonic per author).
    3. Open store A, apply events in original order, snapshot all node content_hashes.
    4. Open store B, apply events in shuffled order (preserving per-author lamport sequence — i.e. only swap events from different authors).
    5. Assert SELECT id, content_hash FROM nodes ORDER BY id is byte-identical between A and B.

GENERATOR DETAILS:
- Use math/rand with a deterministic seed derived from the test iteration index so failures are reproducible.
- Op_type distribution: weighted toward update_field/transition_status (most common); a handful of creates per node, a few deletes.
- Author pool: 3 simulated authors (alice, bob, carol).
- Lamport allocation: each author maintains its own counter; any event from author X has lamport > all prior events from X.

SHUFFLE CONSTRAINT:
- Two events e1, e2 from the same author MUST appear in lamport order.
- Two events from different authors are reorderable.
- Implementation: bucket events by author, then interleave the buckets randomly.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Test passes 50 iterations in -short mode (default).
- Test passes 1000 iterations in CI.
- On failure: print the seed, the original order, the shuffled order, and the diverging node IDs for reproducibility.
nodes.acceptance: Property test green for 1000 seeds in CI; reproducible-on-failure via seed.
nodes.creator: cli

Pattern after sync_chaos_test.go (MTIX-15.2.3).

After kill, parent re-opens DB and asserts: either (a) event in applied_events AND mutation persisted, OR (b) event absent AND mutation absent. Never partial.

EDGE CASES (separate file or appended to sync_apply_test.go):

- TestApply_ReplayAgainstEmptyDB: apply 10 events against fresh DB with no prior nodes (some create, some update). Update on missing node surfaces ErrNotFound (not silently dropped).
