nodes.title: inbox --wait empty-park exit code: field report of exit 0 (seq 209); verify contract + add regression test
nodes.prompt: Field report (seq 209, FR-19 requestor, preview build): 'mtix inbox --agent X --wait' on a FIRST park with an empty inbox returned exit 0 with an empty JSON [] instead of the documented exit 5 (exitCodeInboxEmpty). INVESTIGATION so far: the exit-5 contract IS implemented and correct -- runInbox (cmd/mtix/inbox.go:107-110) returns errInboxWaitEmpty when 'wait && len(events)==0'; exitcode.go:45 maps it to 5; main.go:34 silences its message. Reproduced GREEN (exit 5) on both preview binaries (a393687 and the requestor's 1fe0547) and on current main, for every empty-park variant tried: --timeout 0, -1, 1; JSON and human output; first park after a fresh init. Could NOT reproduce exit 0. HYPOTHESIS: the observed exit 0 is an artifact of the Claude harness reaping a long background CLI park (see the sibling ticket), so the exit code the agent captured was the harness wrapper's, not the CLI's -- not a CLI contract bug. To close: (1) get the EXACT seq-209 invocation (flags, timeout, whether run as a harness background task) and try to reproduce; (2) regardless, add a regression test that pins the contract at the cmd level -- assert 'inbox --wait --timeout 1 --json' on an empty inbox exits 5 and prints [] -- so it cannot silently regress; a store-level InboxWait timeout test already implies it but the cmd exit-code mapping is untested. Files: cmd/mtix/inbox.go, cmd/mtix/exitcode.go, cmd/mtix/main.go.
nodes.acceptance: A cmd-level regression test asserts exit 5 + empty JSON [] for 'inbox --wait' on an empty timeout, in both --json and human modes. If the exact seq-209 invocation reproduces a real exit-0 path, it is fixed and covered by that test; otherwise the ticket documents that the CLI contract is correct and the exit-0 was a harness-reaping artifact (cross-linked to the MCP-wake docs ticket).
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: FR-19 docs: Claude-agent durable wake path is MCP mtix_inbox_wait, not a backgrounded CLI park (harness reaps parks)
nodes.prompt: Environment finding from the FR-19 field trial (seq 209): a long-running 'mtix inbox --wait --timeout 300' parked as a background process gets REAPED by the Claude harness, so a Claude-based agent cannot rely on a backgrounded CLI park as its durable wake primitive. This is exactly the 'agent that can't hold a socket' case FR-19.5 was designed for: the durable, harness-safe wake path for Claude agents is the MCP tools -- mtix_inbox_wait (long-poll, capped ~60s, RE-INVOKE on empty return), mtix_inbox (list), mtix_inbox_ack -- invoked as ordinary tool calls, not a held process. The CLI --wait primitive remains correct for agents that CAN hold a process (e.g. a shell-loop worker, the aarch64 sandbox). ACTION: document this guidance prominently. Update dist/FR-19-PREVIEW.md (the wake-loop section currently leads with 'mtix inbox --wait' for the worker loop -- add that Claude/harness agents should use mtix_inbox_wait and re-invoke on empty, and that a backgrounded CLI park may be reaped) and the FR-19 feature docs / FR-19.5 section. No code change required unless we also want a doc note in the inbox --wait --help text pointing Claude agents at the MCP tool.
nodes.acceptance: FR-19-PREVIEW.md and the FR-19.5 docs state clearly: Claude/harness-hosted agents should use the MCP mtix_inbox_wait tool (re-invoke on empty) as the durable wake path because a backgrounded CLI --wait park can be reaped; CLI --wait stays documented for agents that can hold a process. No behavioral regression to the CLI.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Hooks: exec fires on the triggering host; daemon-driven dispatch to run exec on a designated host (multi-machine)
nodes.prompt: Field finding (FR-19 exec adapter): hooks dispatch runs in the CLI's post-commit path (PersistentPostRunE -> HooksDispatcher.Dispatch), so adapters -- including exec -- fire on the machine where the triggering mutating command RAN, reading that machine's local journal. Confirmed behavior, not a bug: the exec wake-script executes wherever the CLI happened to post the event. SINGLE-MACHINE topology is fully correct and is the path that matters today -- workers and the poster are the same host, so exec spawns in a working local environment. The wrinkle is MULTI-MACHINE: if machine X posts an event but the wake-script needs to run on machine Y (Y has the toolchain/auth/worker), firing on X is wrong -- observed as an exec that launched but whose spawned action failed because X's environment lacked what the script needed. THE ANSWER (already gestured at -- hooks_dispatch.go:24 notes dispatch also runs 'periodically in the daemon'): daemon-driven dispatch on a DESIGNATED HOST. A dispatch daemon on host Y consumes the journal and fires exec there, regardless of which machine posted the event, instead of exec firing wherever the CLI ran. DESIGN INTERACTIONS to spec: (1) the synced-event guard -- a designated-host daemon must act on events that ORIGINATED elsewhere, i.e. synced events, so those hooks need include-synced (opt-in) while still guarding against every host re-firing; exactly one designated host fires, not all pullers. (2) exec content-hash trust is LOCAL -- trust is pinned on the designated host, so the daemon host is where the operator trusts hooks.yaml (+ MTIX-49's target-script pin if that lands). (3) rate-limit/loop guards move with the daemon. Scope: design + implement daemon-host-designated exec dispatch; keep CLI-local dispatch as the default for single-machine. No change to single-machine behavior.
nodes.acceptance: A designated host can run hook exec dispatch: exec fires on that host for matching events regardless of which machine posted them, with include-synced hooks acting exactly once (the designated host), not once per puller. Synced-guard, local exec-trust, and rate-limit interactions are specified and tested. Single-machine CLI-local dispatch is unchanged and remains the default. No exec fires on a non-designated host when daemon dispatch is configured.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Dispatcher: separate synced-dispatch path + cursor; local dispatch never fires synced (designated-host core)
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Wire designated synced-dispatch into the sync daemon (--dispatch-hooks) + docs
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: MCP server does not dispatch hooks on mutations — wire Dispatch into the on-commit path (host-side exec cold-start)
nodes.prompt: GAP: hook dispatch (service.HooksDispatcher.Dispatch) is invoked in exactly two places — the CLI post-command path (cmd/mtix/root.go PersistentPostRunE) and the sync daemon's designated synced-dispatch (cmd/mtix/sync_daemon.go, MTIX-52). The long-running MCP server (cmd/mtix/mcp.go runMCP) NEVER dispatches hooks: its only on-commit wiring is wireMirrorExporter (store.SetOnCommit -> tasks.json export + rolling backup), not hooks. CONSEQUENCE: when an agent drives mtix through the MCP server (e.g. a desktop-app local connector running host-side), its mutations commit and mirror, but NO hooks fire — so an exec hook cannot cold-start a worker on the host. This blocks the 'run the mtix MCP server host-side so exec fires there' path (the connector alternative to daemon-driven dispatch, MTIX-52). FIX: dispatch hooks after each committed MCP mutation. The clean seam is the same store.SetOnCommit used by the mirror exporter — register a second on-commit callback that runs app.hooksDisp.Dispatch(ctx) (the LOCAL path: MCP mutations are local events on the server host, so Dispatch, not DispatchSynced). Wire it in runMCP alongside wireMirrorExporter. This gives IMMEDIATE host-side firing (better than the daemon's poll). exec still requires local content-hash trust on the server host (MTIX-49), the per-node rate limit and via-hook guard still apply, and Dispatch never blocks/fails a mutation. Consider whether the same wiring belongs in  (the HTTP/gRPC server) for parity. DESIGN NOTE / posture: this makes a persistent server auto-fire exec on agent mutations — acceptable because exec is off unless locally trusted, but call it out in docs. Files: cmd/mtix/mcp.go, cmd/mtix/mirror_wiring.go (or a sibling), possibly cmd/mtix/serve.go.
nodes.acceptance: A committed mutation made through the MCP server fires matching LOCAL hooks (inbox/webhook/append-file/exec subject to local trust), verified by a test that drives an MCP tool mutation and asserts the hook delivered (e.g. inbox delivery recorded / exec-trusted hook ran). Hook dispatch never blocks or fails an MCP tool call. Rate-limit, via-hook, and exec-trust guards all still apply. tasks.json mirror + backup behavior unchanged. No hooks fire twice for one mutation. Docs note that a host-side MCP server fires local hooks (incl. exec when trusted).
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Filesystem-safety preflight: detect unsafe (FUSE/network/virtualized) filesystems and prevent WAL/shm corruption
nodes.prompt: From the 2026-07-11 RCA (preserved in internal-docs/). Root cause: a .mtix SQLite DB accessed over a FUSE-passthrough shared folder by concurrent multi-process access corrupts at runtime — SQLite WAL needs true shared-memory mmap of the -shm wal-index plus faithful POSIX locking/fsync, which FUSE/network filesystems do not provide, so processes run on divergent wal-index views (malformed/lost writes). mtix forces journal_mode=WAL unconditionally in internal/store/sqlite/store.go openDB() with NO filesystem-type detection (statfs is used only for free space in diskspace_unix.go). DELIVERABLES: P0 filesystem-safety preflight in Store.New — statfs the DB dir and classify FS type (macOS f_fstypename in {fuse,macfuse,osxfuse,nfs,smbfs,webdav}; Linux f_type magic {FUSE 0x65735546, NFS 0x6969, SMB/CIFS 0xFF534D42, 9p 0x01021997}); on an unsafe FS refuse to open by default with an actionable message naming the FS type and the sync-hub topology, overridable by env MTIX_ALLOW_UNSAFE_FS=1. P1 safe-mode fallback when overridden: journal_mode=DELETE/TRUNCATE + locking_mode=EXCLUSIVE + enforce single-process; never WAL on an unsafe FS. P2 runtime shm probe: detect SQLITE_IOERR_SHM* at open and refuse WAL. P3 observability: log corruption detection and every recover/restore with DB path + FS type. P4 docs: never host .mtix on shared/network/virtualized folders; one local .mtix per sandbox synced via the Postgres hub (FR-18). Files: internal/store/sqlite/store.go, new fsdetect_{linux,darwin}.go, diskspace_unix.go. TDD red-first, >=90% coverage, cross-platform build tags (Linux f_type vs darwin f_fstypename).
nodes.acceptance: 1. Opening .mtix on a FUSE/network/virtualized FS is refused by default with a clear error naming the FS type (test via an injected FS classifier). 2. MTIX_ALLOW_UNSAFE_FS opens in safe single-process mode (journal_mode != WAL, locking_mode=EXCLUSIVE), asserted by PRAGMA. 3. Local disk opens in WAL as today (no regression). 4. Corruption/recover events logged with DB path + FS type. 5. Docs updated with topology guidance. 6. Builds+tests pass on darwin + linux. 7. >=90% coverage on new code; full suite + cloud gate green.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Inbox selective ack (per-event ledger): cumulative watermark drops unacked events on out-of-order processing
nodes.prompt: RCA: the per-agent inbox ack is a CUMULATIVE WATERMARK. InboxAck (internal/store/sqlite/inbox.go) does 'cursor = MAX(cursor, seq)' on agent_inbox_cursor, and InboxList returns events with rowid > cursor. So if an agent processes OUT OF ORDER and acks a higher seq while lower seqs are still unprocessed, the watermark jumps past them and InboxList never returns them again — silent, permanent loss. The at-least-once promise only holds for strictly in-order, contiguous acking; any out-of-order or partial processing drops events. There is also no defer path (an agent cannot mark an event 'seen but not done, redeliver later'). FIX (selective ack via a per-event ledger): add table agent_inbox_ack(agent_id TEXT, event_seq INTEGER, acked_at TEXT, PRIMARY KEY(agent_id,event_seq)). Change InboxAck(agent, seq) to record THAT specific seq in the ledger (idempotent), not advance a watermark. InboxList excludes an event when rowid <= watermark OR rowid is in the ledger (keeps the existing agent_inbox_cursor watermark as a backward-compatible floor + a compaction target). Add InboxAckThrough(agent, seq) for the explicit BULK 'ack everything up to N' case (advances the watermark and prunes ledger rows <= N, bounding ledger growth). Defer = simply not acking (the event resurfaces on the next InboxList). Wire CLI 'mtix inbox ack <seq> [<seq>...]' to selective (multiple seqs), add '--through' for the cumulative case; wire MCP mtix_inbox_ack to selective. Files: internal/store/sqlite/inbox.go, schema.go, cmd/mtix/inbox.go, internal/mcp/tools_inbox.go. TDD red-first; the key test reproduces the drop (ack C selectively, assert A and B still delivered).
nodes.acceptance: 1. Acking a higher seq does NOT drop lower unacked events — InboxList still returns them (regression test reproduces the old drop). 2. InboxAck is idempotent and selective (acks exactly the given seq(s)). 3. Existing agent_inbox_cursor watermarks still hide events at/below them (backward compat). 4. InboxAckThrough advances the watermark and prunes the ledger. 5. CLI 'inbox ack' accepts multiple seqs (selective) + '--through'; MCP mtix_inbox_ack is selective. 6. >=90% coverage on new code; full suite + cloud gate green.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: FR-20: Origin-independent hook dispatch (journal-tail dispatcher + daemon-as-service)
nodes.prompt: Full requirement: internal-docs/FR-20-ORIGIN-INDEPENDENT-DISPATCH.md. Make hook dispatch ORIGIN-INDEPENDENT: a hook fires for a journaled event based only on the event being in the journal and the hook not yet having fired for it on this host — never on who wrote it (local CLI, MCP, REST/gRPC, sync-arrived, or another process). Core: replace the local/synced dual-cursor split with a durable per-(hook,event) DISPATCH LEDGER (exactly-once per host, survives restarts/concurrency/out-of-order — same ledger pattern as MTIX-55 selective ack). One journal-tail dispatch path shared by the daemon (continuous pull-then-tail) and the on-commit trigger (MTIX-53, low-latency local). Fleet exactly-once = hook PLACEMENT (an exec hook lives on the worker host only); include-synced becomes a deprecated no-op. Ship the daemon as an OS-agnostic SERVICE (launchd/systemd/Task Scheduler) via 'mtix daemon install'. Add a global --project/-C <dir> flag on ALL commands. Topology: origin-independence is delivered over the SYNC HUB (each host its own local .mtix), NOT a shared mount (which MTIX-54 refuses as corruption-prone) — the feature must not require a shared filesystem. Crash semantics: at-least-once exec with reclaimable leases (no lost wake; idempotent wake scripts). New-hook backfill guard (intro-floor) so a newly added hook fires only on future events. Claude Code channels: support both — the fabric works channel-free (exec cold-start is the baseline release test); an optional mtix-channel push adapter is an additive follow-up, never a dependency. Release test: a sandboxed poster's addressed comment → the worker host's daemon fires the wake exec → worker cold-starts, zero human touches. Child breakdown + edge/corner cases + scenario tests are in the FR doc §11-14. Build generic (no codenames in code/public docs). To be built by a fable-5 agent, then reviewed.
nodes.acceptance: The release chain works headless on the hub topology: an addressed comment posted on host A fires the wake exec EXACTLY ONCE on the designated worker host B (via B's daemon), does not fire on A, does not re-fire on B restart, and re-fires (not lost) after a crash between claim and fire. include-synced still parses (no-op). Global --project works on non-mcp commands. Daemon installs as an OS service and survives reboot. Filesystem safety (MTIX-54) intact — no shared-mount requirement. Full suite + cloud gate green; >=90% coverage on new code.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Dispatch ledger + journal-tail dispatcher (exactly-once per host)
nodes.prompt: Replace hook_dispatch_cursor/hook_synced_cursor with a durable per-(hook,event) dispatch ledger: hook_dispatch_ledger(hook_name, event_seq, fired_at, outcome) PK(hook_name,event_seq). One dispatchTail path for all triggers (daemon tail + on-commit); ledgerClaim via INSERT OR IGNORE = exactly-once primitive; claim-then-fire with lease reclaim (stale 'claimed' rows re-fired after timeout; outcome=error never auto-retried). Per-hook intro-floor so a new hook fires only on future events (backfill:true opt-in). Scan floor as optimization only; pruning never passes a stale claimed row. On-commit trigger scopes by scan window of just-committed rows, not an origin predicate. Reject duplicate hook names at config load. Migrate: seed floor from hook_dispatch_cursor, drop hook_synced_cursor, include-synced becomes documented no-op.
nodes.acceptance: Unit+property tests: exactly-once under concurrent triggers, out-of-order arrival, restart no-refire, crash-between-claim-and-fire re-fires, error-no-retry, intro-floor no-backfill, rename resets floor, duplicate names rejected, prune respects lease. Existing hooks tests green. >=90% coverage on new code.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Exec dispatch-host policy: local knob (any|daemon|off) so exec fires only where designated
nodes.prompt: Fleet requirement: a host-local, never-synced knob controlling WHERE exec hooks fire. Modes: any (default, current behavior), daemon (only a daemon-marked dispatcher runs dispatch passes on this host - CLI/server triggers no-op entirely so they can never claim-and-consume a pair the daemon should fire; floor untouched), off (dispatch runs but the exec adapter is skipped with terminal outcome skipped-policy; other adapters unaffected). Stored in a local file next to the trust hash (.mtix/, untracked), set via 'mtix hooks exec-dispatch [mode]'. Complements per-host trust (skipped-untrusted is terminal per host - an untrusted host never execs), giving explicit intent instead of implicit-by-not-trusting.
nodes.acceptance: daemon mode: non-daemon Dispatch is a no-op (no ledger rows, floor unchanged); daemon dispatcher fires everything. off: exec skipped-policy terminal, inbox/webhook still deliver. CLI get/set round-trips. Docs updated. Suite green.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Safe binary upgrade path: macOS kills in-place-overwritten binaries (SIGKILL/AMFI); daemon KeepAlive amplifies to crash loop
nodes.prompt: Field-hit during FR-20 fleet testing: on Apple Silicon, overwriting an existing mtix binary in place (cp over the file) invalidates the kernel's cached ad-hoc code signature for that inode; every exec is killed with SIGKILL before main (even --version). With 'mtix daemon install' (launchd KeepAlive) the killed binary is respawned forever - a crash loop. Homebrew and go install are safe (new inode/rename); the GitHub-Releases binary-download path has no upgrade guidance. Fix in build+deploy: (1) 'make install' target using install(1) semantics (unlink-then-copy, PREFIX overridable) so the documented path is safe by construction; (2) README Binary Download section: upgrade-safe commands (install -m 0755 / rm-then-cp; never cp over an existing binary on macOS) + note to run 'mtix daemon start' after upgrading so the service picks up the new binary; (3) USERMANUAL 'Upgrading' note under daemon-as-service; (4) RELEASE-CHECKLIST post-release smoke includes the macOS overwrite-upgrade path; (5) 'mtix daemon install' output reminds how to upgrade.
nodes.acceptance: make install PREFIX=<tmp> installs a runnable binary and is repeat-safe over an existing one on macOS; docs updated in all four places; suite+lint green.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Origin-independent daemon (mtix daemon): pull-then-tail loop
nodes.prompt: Promote 'sync daemon --dispatch-hooks' into first-class 'mtix daemon': continuous pull-from-hub-then-dispatchTail loop (1-5s); fires hooks for any journaled event regardless of origin (local CLI, MCP, sync-arrived, cross-process); single-instance PID lock; never blocks or exits on transient errors; structured logging of every firing and skip; works with no hub configured (local tail only). Deprecated alias kept for one release.
nodes.acceptance: Daemon fires sync-arrived and cross-process events exactly once via the 56.1 ledger; restart-safe; second instance exits cleanly; alias works; tests green.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Daemon-as-service install (launchd/systemd/Task Scheduler)
nodes.prompt: mtix daemon install|status|start|stop|uninstall generating and registering an OS service: launchd (darwin), systemd user unit (linux), Task Scheduler (windows). Boot-start + crash-restart. Idempotent install/uninstall. Upgrades the existing --install stub.

nodes.title: Global -C/--project-dir flag on all commands
nodes.prompt: Persistent root flag -C/--project-dir <dir> with git -C semantics (effective working directory change) so any command targets a named .mtix without cd. Migrate mcp's local --project/-C to the global flag keeping a hidden deprecated alias. --project (project prefix) untouched on create/list/sync commands.

nodes.title: e2e release test + three-agent scenario regression test
nodes.prompt: Two parts. (a) Cross-host chain on live Postgres hub: store A posts addressed comment -> A pushes -> host B daemon pulls+tails -> exec fires exactly once on B, not on A; restart no-refire; crash injection between claim and fire re-fires (at-least-once). (b) Three-agent single-host scenario as a permanent regression guard: planner posts comment --to developer on a task -> developer inbox delivery -> channel adapter emits the notification frame (fake stdio client) carrying the comment -> ack clears it -> developer posts --to tester -> same chain -> tester replies. Also assert cold-start fallback: no presence -> exec fires with prompt-format payload; presence held -> exec skipped. Runs in the standard suite.
nodes.acceptance: Both chains automated and green in CI; scenario test runs on every build; release gate wired to (a).
nodes.creator: cli

SECURITY.md: per-host exec trust, placement = fleet designation, addressed comments are prompt input. CHANGELOG: include-synced behavior change (hooks now fire on sync-arrived events, deduped per host). Generic; no codenames.

nodes.title: Channel adapter: internal/channel Go module + mtix mcp --channel-agent
nodes.prompt: Pluggable internal/channel package: Adapter interface over an inbox Source (long-poll past ack cursor, at-least-once). First adapter 'claudecode': declares capabilities.experimental['claude/channel'] and emits notifications/claude/channel (content + identifier-keyed meta) over the existing stdio MCP server when --channel-agent <id> is set; instructions string directs handle -> ack -> reply via existing MCP tools. Registers/heartbeats agent presence (session primitive) for wake-script arbitration. Protocol constants isolated in one file with a pinned stdio contract test (research-preview churn early-warning). Non-gating for release; docs mark experimental (dev flag/org allowlist, Anthropic auth, v2.1.80+).

nodes.title: Prompt-terminated delivery: inbox --format prompt|context + wake template
nodes.prompt: mtix inbox --format prompt|context emitting agent-ready text (task refs, comment bodies, sender) for wake scripts and harness context-injection hooks; reads stay non-destructive (ack remains explicit). Reference wake-script template uses it: check presence -> check inbox non-empty -> launch harness CLI with the prompt payload. Cross-link plugin-side hook work (separate plugin effort, not gated here).
nodes.acceptance: Format output stable and tested; template documented and used by the 56.5 scenario test.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Async exec dispatch: mutation path must not block on hook commands (FR-19 contract fix)
nodes.prompt: Field finding (fleet acceptance run): ExecAdapter.Deliver uses cmd.Run(), which WAITS for the hook command to finish (up to timeout-seconds, default 30s). The CLI post-command trigger and the server on-commit trigger therefore block the mutation path for the duration of a wake script — violating FR-19's 'dispatch runs after commit and never blocks the mutation' contract. Interim field fix: self-detaching wake scripts. Upstream fix (decide before build): spawn the exec DETACHED (Setsid, no wait) and record ledger outcome=delivered on successful SPAWN; the exit code becomes the script's own responsibility (a wake's true success signal is the inbox getting acked, which the fabric already tracks). Rationale: waiting in a goroutine instead breaks in ephemeral CLI processes (parent exits -> outcome stays claimed -> lease re-fire -> systematic double wake). Keep claim-then-spawn ordering so crash-before-spawn still reclaims (at-least-once). hook_log records spawn success/failure; docs + CHANGELOG note that non-zero script exits are no longer visible in hook_log (script should report its own failures). Update the §7 crash-semantics doc and the wake-script template note.
nodes.acceptance: 1. A slow exec hook (sleep 5) adds <100ms to the mutating CLI command and to an MCP tool call. 2. Claim-then-spawn: crash before spawn still re-fires via lease; successful spawn is terminal delivered. 3. Spawn failure (missing script) records outcome=error, never auto-retried. 4. e2e scenario + release-gate tests stay green. 5. Docs/CHANGELOG updated.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Corruption hardening: enforce one-local-.mtix-per-context; detect cross-context/shared-mount access even when local FS looks safe
nodes.prompt: RCA: internal-docs/2026-07-12-recurring-multiagent-corruption-rca.md. Recurring structural corruption under multi-agent load: multiple execution contexts share ONE .mtix SQLite DB over a FUSE passthrough (sandbox->host) and write concurrently; WAL -shm shared memory + locking are incoherent across contexts -> orphaned-page corruption. Fingerprint: 17 orphaned .fuse_hidden -shm files + 605KB uncheckpointed WAL. Fleet runs a pre-MTIX-54 build (no preflight). Data fully recovered via mtix recover (45 nodes). ROOT CAUSE is a topology flaw, not a code bug: SQLite is single-host/single-local-FS; sharing one file across contexts cannot be made safe by detection alone. CURE is FR-20 hub-per-host (each context its own local .mtix; share via the Postgres hub). This ticket is DEFENSE-IN-DEPTH so a shared-file setup fails LOUD instead of corrupting: (1) verify MTIX-54 preflight ships to all consumers; refusal message must name the hub topology. (2) Multi-opener/cross-context detector: at open + periodically, detect that another context uses this DB even when the local FS looks local -- e.g. .fuse_hidden -shm orphans in the DB dir, or a -shm/WAL from an unreachable owner -- and warn/refuse. (3) Optional lightweight single-writer write-lease sentinel in the DB dir so a second writing context is refused (belt-and-suspenders over SQLite locking that FUSE subverts). (4) Docs: 'one local .mtix per machine/sandbox + hub; never a shared mount' as a first-line adoption rule. TDD; generic (no codenames). Cross-links FR-20 (MTIX-56, the structural cure) and MTIX-54 (the guard).
nodes.acceptance: 1. A shared/FUSE .mtix is refused with an actionable, hub-pointing message (extends MTIX-54). 2. Cross-context concurrent access is detected even when the local FS reports local (test via injected .fuse_hidden/-shm signals). 3. Recover + mirror path documented as the always-available data-safety net. 4. Docs promote one-local-.mtix-per-context + hub as the adoption default. 5. Full suite + cloud gate green; >=90% coverage on new code.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Query and notification features for team workflows
nodes.description: Two features enabling agent-driven integration with external tools (Jira, Linear, GitHub Issues) without building specific importers. Agents read external tickets, create mtix tasks, and sync state. These features provide the infrastructure agents need: a change feed for polling and webhooks for push notifications.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Add --changed-since filter to list and query commands
nodes.description: Enables sync agents to poll efficiently: 'what changed since my last check?' Without this, agents must fetch all nodes and diff against their own state. Index on updated_at already exists (idx_nodes_updated).
nodes.prompt: Add --changed-since flag to mtix list and mtix query commands. Accept ISO 8601 timestamp. Implementation: add ChangedSince time.Time field to store.NodeFilter. In internal/store/sqlite/node_list.go buildFilterClauses, add WHERE clause: updated_at > ? with parameterized timestamp value. Also support relative durations (5m, 1h, 24h) parsed in the CLI layer. Add --json support. Files: cmd/mtix/query.go (flag), internal/store/store.go (NodeFilter), internal/store/sqlite/node_list.go (WHERE clause).
nodes.acceptance: 1. mtix list --changed-since 2026-04-06T10:00:00Z returns only nodes modified after that time. 2. mtix list --changed-since 1h returns nodes modified in the last hour. 3. Parameterized query (no SQL injection). 4. Works with --json flag. 5. Works with other filters (--status, --assignee) simultaneously. 6. Test coverage for the new filter.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Add outgoing webhook support for state change notifications
nodes.description: Generic webhook system for push-based notifications. When a node changes state, mtix fires an HTTP POST to configured URLs. Enables event-driven sync with external tools without mtix knowing anything about Jira/Linear/GitHub Issues.
nodes.prompt:
Implementation requirements from security and performance audit:

Config (.mtix/config.yaml):
  webhooks:
    - url: https://example.com/webhook
      secret: hmac-secret
      events: [node.created, node.done, node.cancelled, node.blocked]
      payload: minimal  # or full

Core requirements:
1. ASYNC delivery — dispatch from event broadcaster goroutine, NEVER inside a DB transaction (WH-01)
2. Minimal default payload: {event, node_id, status, project, timestamp}. Full payload (includes title, assignee) only when payload: full is set (WH-02)
3. HMAC-SHA256 signature in X-Mtix-Signature header using per-webhook secret (WH-03)
4. 100ms debounce window — collect events, send as batch array to prevent bulk operation floods (WH-04)
5. Config-file only — not API-configurable (WH-05)
6. Warn at startup if webhook URL is non-HTTPS and non-localhost (WH-06)
7. 5-second timeout per delivery
8. 3 retries with exponential backoff (1s, 5s, 25s), then drop with warning log
9. HTTP client with connection pooling (reuse connections to same URL)

Files: internal/service/config.go (webhook config struct), internal/service/broadcaster.go (webhook dispatch), internal/service/webhook.go (new — HTTP client, HMAC, batching), cmd/mtix/root.go (config loading and validation).
nodes.acceptance: 1. Webhook fires on configured events. 2. Payload matches configured mode (minimal/full). 3. X-Mtix-Signature header present and verifiable. 4. Slow endpoint does not block mtix operations. 5. Bulk decompose (50 children) produces batched webhook, not 50 individual calls. 6. Non-HTTPS non-localhost URL produces startup warning. 7. Failed delivery retries 3 times then logs warning. 8. Config-file only — no API endpoint to add webhooks. 9. Test coverage for delivery, HMAC, batching, timeout, retry.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Align node type hierarchy with industry convention
nodes.description: mtix has Epic below Story which is inverted from Agile/Scrum/Jira/Linear convention. Swap depth-to-type mapping: depth 0 = epic (was story), depth 1 = story (was epic). Issue and micro unchanged. Derive node_type from depth on import for tamper resistance and cross-version compatibility.
nodes.prompt: Three code changes: (1) In internal/model/types.go NodeTypeForDepth, swap case 0 to return NodeTypeEpic and case 1 to return NodeTypeStory. Update constant comments. (2) In internal/store/sqlite/import.go insertExportNode and mergeImportNode, set node_type to NodeTypeForDepth(n.Depth) instead of trusting the file value. (3) Fix 26 test references: 21 NodeTypeStory at depth 0 → NodeTypeEpic, 5 NodeTypeEpic at depth 1 → NodeTypeStory. 132 Issue/Micro refs unchanged. Update README, USERMANUAL, CONTRIBUTING. Regenerate docs.
nodes.acceptance: 1. NodeTypeForDepth(0) returns epic. 2. NodeTypeForDepth(1) returns story. 3. Import derives type from depth, ignores file value. 4. All tests pass. 5. Coverage >= 87%. 6. golangci-lint clean. 7. Build succeeds. 8. Documentation updated.

nodes.title: Skip redundant auto-import via meta table export hash
nodes.description: Auto-import triggers on every command when tasks.json hash differs from stored hash. With multiple agents on the same machine, each mutation triggers an export (hash changes) which causes all other agents to do a full ImportModeReplace — a thundering herd problem. Fix: store last export hash in the meta table. If file hash matches last export hash, the file came from our own export — skip import. External changes (git pull) produce a different hash and import correctly.
nodes.prompt:
TDD approach — write failing test first.

TEST (in internal/service/sync_service_test.go):
TestAutoImport_SkipsWhenFileMatchesOwnExport: Create a sync service, insert nodes, call AutoExport (writes tasks.json + sets meta last_export_hash), then call AutoImport. Import should be skipped because the file hash matches the export hash. Verify by checking that nodes were NOT reimported (no write transaction occurred).

TestAutoImport_ImportsWhenFileChangedExternally: Create a sync service, insert nodes, call AutoExport, then modify tasks.json externally (simulate git pull), then call AutoImport. Import SHOULD trigger because file hash differs from export hash. Verify by checking the imported data matches the modified file.

TestAutoImport_ImportsWhenNoExportHashExists: Fresh database with no last_export_hash in meta. Place a tasks.json file. AutoImport should trigger (fail-safe to current behavior).

TestAutoImport_SkipsWhenSiblingAgentExported: Agent A exports (sets meta hash). Agent B has not exported. Agent B runs AutoImport. Should skip because meta hash matches file hash (Agent A and B share the same DB).

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. In AutoExport (sync_service.go), after writing tasks.json and hash files, write the file hash to meta: store.WriteDB().ExecContext(ctx, INSERT OR REPLACE INTO meta (key, value) VALUES ('last_export_hash', ?), fileHash)

2. In AutoImport (sync_service.go), after detecting file hash differs from stored hash, check meta: SELECT value FROM meta WHERE key = 'last_export_hash'. If file hash matches meta value, log at DEBUG level and return nil (skip import).

3. Fail-safe: if meta read fails or returns no row, proceed with import (current behavior).

Files: internal/service/sync_service.go, internal/service/sync_service_test.go
nodes.acceptance: 1. TestAutoImport_SkipsWhenFileMatchesOwnExport passes. 2. TestAutoImport_ImportsWhenFileChangedExternally passes. 3. TestAutoImport_ImportsWhenNoExportHashExists passes. 4. TestAutoImport_SkipsWhenSiblingAgentExported passes. 5. All existing tests pass. 6. Coverage >= 87%. 7. golangci-lint clean.
nodes.creator: cli
